 CHAPTER XIV . 

 Richard Avenel be in a state of great nervous excitement . he propose 
 to give an entertainment of a kind wholly new to the experience of 
 Screwstown . Mrs. M'Catchley have describe with much eloquence the 
 dejeune dansant of her fashionable friend reside in the elegant 
 suburb of Wimbledon and Fulham . she declare that nothing be so 
 agreeable . she have even say point - blank to Mr. Avenel , " why do not   you 
 give a Dejeune dansant ? " and , therewith , a Dejeune dansant Mr. Avenel 
 resolve to give . 

 the day be fix , and Mr. Avenel enter into all the requisite 
 preparation , with the energy of a man and the providence of a woman . 

 one morning as he stand muse on the lawn , irresolute as to the good 
 site for the tent , Leonard come up to he with an open letter in his 
 hand . 

 " my dear uncle , " say he , softly . 

 " ha ! " exclaim Mr. Avenel , with a start . " ha - well , what now ? " 

 " I have just receive a letter from Mr. Dale . he tell I that my poor 
 mother be very restless and uneasy , because he can not assure she that 
 he have hear from I ; and his letter require an answer . indeed I shall 
 seem very ungrateful to he - to all - if I do not write . " 

 Richard Avenel 's brow meet . he utter an impatient " Pish ! " and turn 
 away . then come back , he fix his clear hawk - like eye on Leonard 's 
 ingenuous countenance , link his arm into his nephew 's , and draw he 
 into the shrubbery . 

 " well , Leonard , " say he , after a pause , " it be time that I should give 
 you some idea of my plan with regard to you . you have see my manner of 
 live - some difference from what you ever see before , I calculate ! now 
 I have give you , what no one give I , a lift in the world ; and where I 
 place you , there you must help yourself . " 

 " such be my duty and my desire , " say Leonard , heartily . " good . you 
 be a clever lad , and a genteel lad , and will do I credit . I have have 
 doubt of what be good for you . at one time I think of send you to 
 college . that , I know ; be Mr. Dale 's wish ; perhaps it be your own . but 
 I have give up that idea ; I have something well for you . you have 
 a clear head for business , and be a capital arithmetician . I think of 
 bring you up to superintend my business ; by and by I will admit you 
 into partnership ; and before you be thirty you will be a rich man . 
 come , do that suit you ? " 

 " my dear uncle , " say Leonard , frankly , but much touch by this 
 generosity , " it be not for I to have a choice . I should have prefer 
 go to college , because there I might gain independence for myself and 
 cease to be a burden on you . moreover , my heart move I to study 
 more congenial with the college than the counting - house . but all this be 
 nothing compare with my wish to be of use to you , and to prove in any 
 way , however feebly , my gratitude for all your kindness . " 

 " you be a good , grateful , sensible lad , " exclaim Richard , heartily ; 
 " and believe I , though I be a rough diamond , I have your true interest 
 at heart . you can be of use to I , and in be so you will well 
 serve yourself . to tell you the truth , I have some idea of change 
 my condition . there be a lady of fashion and quality who , I think , may 
 condescend to become Mrs. Avenel ; and if so , I shall probably reside a 
 great part of the year in London . I do not   want to give up my business . 
 no other investment will yield the same interest . but you can soon learn 
 to superintend it for I , as some day or other I may retire , and then 
 you can step in . once a member of our great commercial class , and with 
 your talent you may be anything , - member of parliament , and after that , 
 minister of State , for what I know . and my wife - hem ! that be to be - have 
 great connection , and you shall marry well ; and - oh , the Avenels will 
 hold their head with the high , after all ! damn the aristocracy ! we 
 clever fellow will be the aristocrat , eh ? " Richard rub his hand . 

 certainly , as we have see , Leonard , especially in his early step 
 to knowledge , have repine at his position in the many degree of life ; 
 certainly he be still ambitious ; certainly he could not now have 
 return contentedly to the humble occupation he have leave ; and woe to 
 the young man who do not hear with a quickened pulse and brightening 
 eye word that promise independence , and flatter with the hope of 
 distinction . still , it be with all the reaction of chill and mournful 
 disappointment that Leonard , a few hour after this dialogue with 
 his uncle , find himself alone in the field , and ponder over 
 the prospect before he . he have set his heart upon complete his 
 intellectual education , upon develop those power within he which 
 yearn for an arena of literature , and revolt from the routine of 
 trade . 

 but to his credit be it say , that he vigorously resist this natural 
 disappointment , and by degree school himself to look cheerfully on 
 the path impose on his duty , and sanction by the manly sense that be 
 at the core of his character . 

 I believe that this self - conquest show that the boy have true genius . 
 the false genius would have write sonnet and despair . 

 but still , Richard Avenel leave his nephew sadly perplex as to 
 the knotty question from which their talk on the future have 
 diverge , - namely , should he write to the parson , and assure the fear 
 of his mother ? how do so without Richard 's consent , when Richard have on 
 a former occasion so imperiously declare that , if he do , it would 
 lose his mother all that Richard intend to settle on she ? while he be 
 debate this matter with his conscience , lean against a stile that 
 interrupt a path to the town , Leonard Fairfield be startle by an 
 exclamation . he look up , and beheld Mr. Sprott the tinker . 




 CHAPTER XV . 

 the tinker , blacker and grimmer than ever , star hard at the alter

  " Pride in his port , defiance in his eye . " 

 Mr. Avenel 's finger itch to knock the tinker 's villanous hat off his

 " a tinker ! " he cry , - " that be a vagrant ; and I be a magistrate , and I have

 crakter of the young gent I see you talk with just now ; he know I . " 

 " what ! my nephew know you ? " 

 " W - hew , " whistle the tinker , " your nephew be it , sir ? I have a great 
 respek for your family . I ' ve   know Mrs. Fairfilt the vashervoman this 
 many a year . I ' umbly ax your pardon . " and he take off his hat this

 Mr. Avenel turn red and white in a breath . he growl out something

 he have watch Leonard , and then dog the uncle as he have dog the 
 nephew . I do not   presume to say that there be cause and effect in what 
 happen that night , but it be what be call " a curious coincidence " 
 that that night one of Richard Avenel 's rick be set on fire , and that 
 that day he have call Mr. Sprott an incendiary . Mr. Sprott be a man of 
 a very high spirit , and do not forgive an insult easily . his nature be 
 inflammatory , and so be that of the lucifer which he always carry 
 about he , with his tract and glue - pot . 

 the next morning there be an inquiry make for the tinker , but he have 
 disappear from the neighbourhood . 




 CHAPTER XVI . 

 it be a fortunate thing that the dejeune dansant so absorb Mr. 
 Richard Avenel 's thought that even the conflagration of his rick 
 could not scare away the graceful and poetic image connect with that 
 pastoral festivity . he be even loose and careless in the question he 
 put to Leonard about the tinker ; nor do he send justice in pursuit 
 of that itinerant trader ; for , to say truth , Richard Avenel be a man 
 accustomed to make enemy amongst the low order ; and though he 
 suspect Mr. Sprott of destroy his rick , yet , when he once set about 
 suspecting , he find he have quite as good cause to suspect fifty other 
 person . how on earth could a man puzzle himself about rick and tinker 
 when all his care and energy be devoted to a dejeune dansant ? it 
 be a maxim of Richard Avenel 's , as it ought to be of every clever 
 man , " to do one thing at a time ; " and therefore he postpone all other 
 consideration till the dejeune dansant be fairly do with . amongst 
 these consideration be the letter which Leonard wish to write to 
 the parson . " wait a bit , and we will both write ! " say Richard , 
 good - humouredly , " the moment the dijeune dansant be over ! " 

 it must be own that this fete be no ordinary provincial ceremonial . 
 Richard Avenel be a man to do a thing well when he set about it , - 

  " he souse the cabbage with a bounteous heart . " 

 by little and little his first notion have expand , till what have 
 be mean to be only neat and elegant now embrace the costly and 
 magnificent . artificer accustom to dejeune dansant come all the

 CHAPTER XVII . 

 the Great Day arrive at last ; and Mr. Richard Avenel , from his 
 dressing - room window , look on the scene below as Hannibal or Napoleon 
 look from the Alps on Italy . it be a scene to gratify the thought 
 of conquest , and reward the labour of ambition . place on a little 
 eminence stand the singer from the mountain of the Tyrol , their

 the day be propitious , - not a cloud in the sky . the musician

 there be a vast deal of character in the way that a man perform that

 but the rest of Mr. Avenel 's toilet be not complete with correspondent

 and this be his garb . on such occasion , what epic poet would not

 his surtout - in modern phrase his frockcoat - be blue , a rich blue , a

 boot . " a blue and white silk cravat , tie loose and debonair ; an ample

 and , with his light , firm , well - shape figure , his clear complexion , his 
 keen , bright eye , and feature that bespoke the courage , precision , and 
 alertness of his character , - that be to say , feature bold , not large , 
 well - define , and regular , - you might walk long through town or country 
 before you would see a handsomer speciman of humanity than our friend 
 Richard Avenel . 

 handsome , and feel that he be handsome ; rich , and feel that he 
 be rich ; lord of the fete , and feel that he be lord of the fete , 
 Richard Avenel step out upon his lawn . 

 and now the dust begin to rise along the road , and carriage and gig

 Richard Avenel be not quite at his ease at first in receive his

 and then ! ah , then ! moment so meet for the sweet question of question ,

 there be certain sound and certain sight - the one indistinct ,

 " God bless my soul ! " say Richard Avenel . 




 CHAPTER XVIII . 

 and such a woman ! 

 she have on a cotton gown , - very neat , I dare say , for an 
 under - housemaid ; and such thick shoe ! she have on a little black straw

 he lift abruptly his handsome masculine head , and look round

 " you impudent , ungrateful , audacious - drab ! " 

 yes , drab be the word . I be shocked to say it , but the duty of a

 the poor woman 's voice be so raise and so shrill , that any other and

 " ten thousand devil ! be you mad ? - or do you want to drive I mad ? you

 CHAPTER XIX . 

 Brief as have be his absence , the host could see that , in the interval , 
 a great and notable change have come over the spirit of his company . some 
 of those who live in the town be evidently prepare to return home 
 on foot ; those who live at a distance , and whose carriage   have be 
 send away , and order to return at a fix hour   have not yet arrive , 
 be gather together in small knot and group ; all look sullen and 
 displease , and all instinctively turn from their host as he pass 
 they by . they feel they have be lecture , and they be more put out 
 than Richard himself . they do not know if they might not be lecture 
 again . this vulgar man , of what might he not be capable ? Richard 's 
 shrewd sense comprehend in an instant all the difficulty of his 
 position ; but he walk on deliberately and directly towards Mrs. 
 M'Catchley , who be stand near the grand marquee with the Pompleys 
 and the dean 's lady . as those personage see he make thus boldly 
 towards they , there be a flutter . " hang the fellow ! " say the 
 colonel , intrench himself in his stock , " he be come here . low and 
 shocking - what shall we do ? let we stroll on . " but Richard throw himself 
 in the way of the retreat . " Mrs. M'Catchley , " say he , very gravely , and

 the poor widow look very much discompose . Mrs. Pompley pull she 
 by the sleeve . Richard still stand gaze into her face , with his arm

 " monstrous impudent ! " cry the colonel .

 know how to give he a lesson . " 

 " Madam , " say Richard , as soon as he and his companion be out of

 " on I ? " 

 " on you , and you alone . you have influence with all those people , and 
 a word from you will effect what I desire . Mrs. M'Catchley , " add 
 Richard , with a solemnity that be actually impose , " I flatter myself

 any other soul in these ground ; will you do I this favour , ay or no ? " 

 " what be it , Mr. Avenel ? " ask Mrs. M'Catchley , much disturbed , and 
 somewhat soften , - for she be by no mean a woman without feel ; 
 indeed , she consider herself nervous . 

 " get all your friend - all the company , in short - to come back into the 
 tent for refreshment , for anything . I want to say a few word to they . " 

 " bless I ! Mr. Avenel - a few word ! " cry the widow , " but that be just 
 what they be all afraid of . you must pardon I , but you really can not   ask 
 people to a dejeune dansant , and then - scold them ! " 

 " I be not go to scold they , " say Air . Avenel , very seriously , - " upon 
 my honour , I be not . I be go to make all right , and I even hope

 " lady and Gentlemen , " say he , " I have take the liberty to invite you 
 once more into this tent , in order to ask you to sympathize with I upon 
 an occasion which take we all a little by surprise to - day . 

 " of course , you all know I be a new man , - the maker of my own fortune . " 

 a great many head bow involuntarily . the word be say manfully , 
 and there be a general feeling of respect . " probably , too , " resume Mr. 
 Avenel , " you may know that I be the son of very honest tradespeople . I 
 say honest , and they be not ashamed of I ; I say tradespeople , and I be

 her son to educate and bring up . but I do not tell she where he be , 
 nor even that I have return from America ; I wish to choose my own 
 time for that , when I could give she the surprise , not only of a rich

 manner and education can make one . well , the poor dear woman have find

 moment , and I be wrong to say otherwise , yet I be sure I do not   judge

 must feel who part from each other when they be boy and girl . to 
 I "   and Richard give a great gulp , for he feel that a great gulp alone 
 could swallow the abominable lie he be about to utter   - " to I this have

 I have   say . and wish that you may be all as happy in your family as I

 there be a universal applause when Richard sit down ; and so well in

 Whiz go a fresh bottle of champagne . 

 " I be not accustomed to public speaking , but I could not repress my

 " how shall I thank you for the favour you have do I ? "
