 CHAPTER VIII . 

 Twilight be dark in the room to which Beatrice have conduct Violante . 
 a great change have come over Beatrice . humble and weeping , she kneel 
 beside Violante , hide her face , and implore pardon . and Violante , 
 strive to resist the terror for which she now see such cause as no 
 woman - heart can defy , still seek to soothe , and still sweetly assure 
 forgiveness . 

 Beatrice have learn , after quick and fierce question , which at last 
 compel the answer that clear away every doubt , that her jealousy 
 have be groundless , that she have no rival in Violante . from that moment 
 the passion that have make she the tool of guilt abruptly vanish , and 
 her conscience startle she with the magnitude of her treachery . perhaps 
 have Violante 's heart be wholly free , or she have be of that mere 
 commonplace , girlish character which woman like Beatrice be apt to 
 despise , the marchesa 's affection for Peschiera , and her dread of he , 
 might have make her try to persuade her young kinswoman at least to 
 receive the count 's visit , - at least to suffer he to make his own 
 excuse , and plead his own cause . but there have be a loftiness of 
 spirit in which Violante have first defy the mareliesa 's question , 
 follow by such generous , exquisite sweetness , when the girl perceive 
 how that wild heart be sting and madden , and such purity of mournful 
 candour when she have overcome her own virgin bashfulness sufficiently to 
 undeceive the error she detect , and confess where her own affection 
 be place , that Beatrice bow before she as mariner of old to some 
 fair saint that have allay the storm . 

 " I have deceive you ! " she cry , through her sob ; " but I will now save 
 you at any cost . have you be as I deem , - the rival who have despoil 
 all the hope of my future life , - I could without remorse have be 
 the accomplice I be pledge to be . but now you - oh , you , so good and 
 so noble - you can never , be the bride of Peschiera . Nay , start not ; he 
 shall renounce his design forever , or I will go myself to our emperor , 
 and expose the dark secret of his life . return with I quick to the 
 home from which I ensnare you . " 

 Beatrice 's hand be on the door while she speak . suddenly her face fall , 
 her lip grow white ; the door be lock from without . she call , - no 
 one answer ; the bell - pull in the room give no sound ; the window be 
 high and barred , - they do not look on the river , nor the street , but on 
 a close , gloomy , silent yard , high blank wall all round it ; no one to 
 hear the cry of distress , ring it ever so loud and sharp . 

 Beatrice divine that she herself have be no less ensnare than her 
 companion ; that Peschiera , distrustful of her firmness in evil , have 
 preclude she from the power of reparation . she be in a house only 
 tenant by his hireling . not a hope to save Violante from a fate that 
 now appal she seem to remain . thus , in incoherent self - reproach 
 and frenzied tear , Beatrice kneel beside her victim , communicate more 
 and more the terror that she feel , as the hour roll on , and the room 
 darken , till it be only by the dull lamp which gleam through the 
 grimy window from the yard without , that each see the face of the 
 other . 

 Night come on ; they hear a clock from some distant church strike 
 the hour . the dim fire have long since burn out , and the air become 
 intensely cold . no one break upon their solitude , - not a voice be hear 
 in the house . they feel neither cold nor hunger , - they feel but the 
 solitude , and the silence , and the dread of something that be to come . 

 at length , about midnight , a bell ring at the street door ; then there 
 be the quick sound of step , of sullen bolt withdraw , of low , 
 murmured voice . Light stream through the chink of the door to the 
 apartment , the door itself open . two Italians bear taper enter , 
 and the Count di Peschiera follow . 

 Beatrice spring up , and rush towards her brother . he lay his hand 
 gently on her lip , and motion to the Italians to withdraw . they 
 place the light on the table , and vanish without a word . 

 Peschiera then , put aside his sister , approach Violante . 

 " Fair kinswoman , " say he , with an air of easy but resolute assurance , 
 " there be thing which no man can excuse , and no woman can pardon , 
 unless that love , which be beyond all law , suggest excuse for the one , 
 and obtain pardon for the other . in a word , I have swear to win you , 
 and I have have no opportunity to woo . fear not ; the bad that can 
 befall you be to be my bride ! stand aside , my sister , stand aside . " 

 " Giulio Franzini , I stand between you and she ; you shall strike I to 
 the earth before you can touch even the hem of her robe ! " 

 " what , my sister ! you turn against I ? " 

 " and unless you instantly retire and leave she free , I will unmask you 
 to the emperor . " 

 " too late , mon enfant ! you will sail with we . the effect you may 
 need for the voyage be already on board . you will be witness to our 
 marriage , and by a holy son of the Church . then tell the emperor what 
 you will . " 

 with a light and sudden exertion of his strength , the count put away 
 Beatrice , and fall on his knee before Violante , who , draw to her full 
 height , death - like pale , but untrembling , regard he with unutterable 
 disdain . 

 " you scorn I now , " say he , throw into his feature an expression 
 of humility and admiration , " and I can not wonder at it . but , believe 
 I , that until the scorn yield to a kind sentiment , I will take no 
 advantage of the power I have gain over your fate . " 

 " power ! " say Violante , haughtily . " you have ensnare I into this 
 house , you have gain the power of a day ; but the power over my 
 fate , - no ! " 

 " you mean that your friend have discover your disappearance , and be 
 on your track . fair one , I provide against your friend , and I defy all 
 the law and police of England . the vessel that will bear you from these 
 shore wait in the river hard by . Beatrice , I warn you , - be still , 
 unhand I . in that vessel will be a priest who shall join our hand , but 
 not before you will recognize the truth , that she who fly with Giulio 
 Peschiera must become his wife or quit he as the disgrace of her House , 
 and the scorn of her sex . " 

 " o villain ! villain ! " cry Beatrice . 

 " Peste , my sister , gentle word . you , too , would marry . I tell no tale 
 of you . Signorina , I grieve to threaten force . give I your hand ; we 
 must be go . " 

 Violante elude the clasp that would have profane she , and dart 
 across the room , open the door , and close it hastily behind she . 
 Beatrice clung firmly to the count to detain he from pursuit . but just 
 without the door , close , as if listen to what pass within , stand a 
 man wrap from head to foot in a large boat cloak . the ray of the lamp 
 that beam on the man glitter on the barrel of a pistol which he hold 
 in his right hand . 

 " Hist ! " whisper the man in English , and pass his arm round she ; " in 
 this house you be in that ruffian 's power ; out of it , safe . ah , I be by 
 your side , - I , Violante ! " 

 the voice thrill to Violante 's heart . she start , look up , but 
 nothing be see of the man 's face , what with the hat and cloak , save a 
 mass of raven curl , and a beard of the same hue . 

 the count now throw open the door , drag after he his sister , who 
 still cling round he . 

 " ha , that be well ! " he cry to the man , in Italian . " bear the lady 
 after I , gently ; but if she attempt to cry out , why , force enough to 
 silence she , not more . as for you , Beatrice , traitress that you be , I 
 could strike you to the earth , but - no , this suffice . " he catch 
 his sister in his arm as he speak , and regardless of her cry and 
 struggle , spring down the stair . 

 the hall be crowd with fierce , swarthy man . the count turn to 
 one of they , and whisper ; in an instant the marchesa be seize and 
 gag . the count cast a look over his shoulder ; Violante be close 
 behind , support by the man to whom Peschiera have consign she , and 
 who be point to Beatrice , and appear warn Violante against 
 resistance . 

 Violante be silent , and seem resign . peschiera smile cynically , 
 and , precede by some of his hireling , who hold torch , descend a 
 few step that lead to an abrupt landing - place between the hall and the 
 basement story . there a small door stand open , and the river flow 
 close by . a boat be moor on the bank , round which group four man , 
 who have the air of foreign sailor . at the appearance of Peschiera , 
 three of these man spring into the boat , and get ready their oar . the 
 fourth carefully re - adjust a plank throw from the boat to the wharf , 
 and offer his arm obsequiously to Peschiera . the count be the first 
 to enter , and , hum a gay opera air , take his place by the helm . 
 the two female be next lift in , and Violante feel her hand 
 press almost convulsively by the man who stand by the plank . the rest 
 follow , and in another minute the boat bound swiftly over the wave 
 towards a vessel that lie several furlong adown the river , and apart 
 from all the mean craft that crowd the stream . the star struggle 
 pale through the foggy atmosphere ; not a word be hear within the 
 boat , - no sound save the regular splash of the oar . the count pause 
 from his lively tune , and gather round he the ample fold of his fur 
 pelisse , seem absorb in thought . even by the imperfect light of the 
 star , Peschiera 's face wear an air of sovereign triumph . the result 
 have justify that careless and insolent confidence in himself and in 
 fortune , which be the most prominent feature in the character of the 
 man , who , both bravo and gamester , have play against the world with 
 his rapi in one hand and cog dice in the other . Violante , once in 
 a vessel fill by his own man , be irretrievably in his power . even 
 her father must feel grateful to learn that the captive of Peschiera have 
 save name and repute in become Peschiera 's wife . even the pride of 
 sex in Violante herself must induce she to confirm what Peschiera , of 
 course , intend to state , - namely , that she be a willing partner in 
 a bridegroom 's scheme of flight towards the altar rather than the poor 
 victim of a betrayer , and receive his hand but from his mercy . he 
 see his fortune secure , his success envy , his very character 
 rehabilitate by his splendid nuptial . ambition begin to mingle with 
 his dream of pleasure and pomp . what post in the Court or the State too 
 high for the aspiration of one who have evince the most incontestable 
 talent for active life , - the talent to succeed in all that the will have 
 undertake ? thus muse the count , half - forgetful of the present , and 
 absorb in the golden future , till he be arouse by a loud hail from 
 the vessel and the bustle on board the boat , as the sailor catch at 
 the rope fling forth to they . 

 he then rise and move towards Violante . but the man who be still in 
 charge of she pass the count lightly , half - lead , half - carry his 
 passive prisoner . " Pardon , Excellency , " say the man , in Italian , " but 
 the boat be crowd , and rock so much that your aid would but disturb 
 our footing . " before Peschiera could reply , Violante be already on the 
 step of the vessel , and the count pause till , with elated smile , 
 he see she safely stand on the deck . Beatrice follow , and then 
 Peschiera himself ; but when the Italians in his train also throng 
 towards the side of the boat , two of the sailor get before they , and 
 let go the rope , while the other two ply their oar vigorously , 
 and pull back towards shore . the Italians burst into an amazed and 
 indignant volley of execration . " silence , " say the sailor who have 
 stand by the plank , " we obey order . if you be not quiet , we shall 
 upset the boat . we can swim ; Heaven and Monsignore San Giacomo pity you 
 if you can not ! " 

 meanwhile , as Peschiera leap upon deck , a flood of light pour upon 
 he from lift torch . that light stream full on the face and form 
 of a man of command stature , whose arm be around Violante , and whose 
 dark eye flash upon the count more luminously than the torch . on 
 one side this man stand the austrian prince ; on the other side   a cloak , 
 and a profusion of false dark lock , at his foot   stand Lord L'Estrange , 
 his arm fold , and his lip curve by a smile in which the ironical 
 humour native to the man be temper with a calm and supreme disdain . 
 the count strove to speak , but his voice falter . 

 all around he look ominous and hostile . he see many italian face , 
 but they scowl at he with vindictive hate ; in the rear be english 
 mariner , peer curiously over the shoulder of the foreigner , and 
 with a broad grin on their open countenance . suddenly , as the count 
 thus stand perplexed , cower , stupefy , there burst from all 
 the Italians present a hoot of unutterable scorn , " Il traditore ! il 
 traditore ! "   the traitor ! the traitor ! 

 the count be brave , and at the cry he lift his head with a certain 
 majesty . 

 at that moment Harley , raise his hand as if to silence the hoot , come 
 forth from the group by which he have be hitherto stand , and towards 
 he the count advance with a bold stride . 

 " what trick be this ? " he say , in French , fiercely . " I divine that it be 
 you whom I can single out for explanation and atonement . " 

 " Pardieu , Monsieur le Comte , " answer Harley , in the same language , 
 which lend itself so well to polished sarcasm and high - breed enmity , 
 " let we distinguish . Explanation should come from I , I allow ; but 
 atonement I have the honour to resign to yourself . this vessel - " 

 " be mine ! " cry the count . " those man , who insult I , should be in my 
 pay . " 

 " the man in your pay , Monsieur le Comte , be on shore , drink success 
 to your voyage . but , anxious still to procure you the gratification of 
 be amongst your own countryman , those whom I have take into my pay 
 be still well Italians than the pirate whose place they supply ; 
 perhaps not such good sailor ; but then I have take the liberty to add 
 to the equipment of a vessel which cost I too much to risk lightly , 
 some stout english seaman , who be mariner more practised than even 
 your pirate . your grand mistake , Monsieur le Comte , be in think that 
 the ' Flying Dutchman ' be yours . with many apology for interfere with 
 your intention to purchase it , I beg to inform you that Lord Spendquick 
 have kindly sell it to I . nevertheless , Monsieur le Comte , for the next 
 few week I place it - man and all - at your service . " 

 Peschiera smile scornfully . 

 " I thank your Lordship ; but since I presume that I shall no long have 
 the travel companion who alone could make the voyage attractive , I 
 shall return to shore , and will simply request you to inform I at what 
 hour you can receive the friend whom I shall depute to discuss that 
 part of the question yet untouched , and to arrange that the atonement , 
 whether it be due from I or yourself , may be render as satisfactory 
 as you have condescend to make the explanation . " 

 " let not that vex you , Monsieur le Comte ; the atonement be , in much , 
 make already ; so anxious have I be to forestall all that your nice 
 sense of honour would induce so complete a gentleman to desire . you have 
 ensnare a young heiress , it be true ; but you see that it be only to 
 restore she to the arm of her father . you have juggle an illustrious 
 kinsman out of his heritage ; but you have voluntarily come on board this 
 vessel , first , to enable his Highness the Prince Von - - - , of whose 
 rank at the Austrian Court you be fully aware , to state to your emperor 
 that he himself have be witness of the manner in which you interpret 
 his Imperial Majesty 's assent to your nuptial with a child of one of 
 the first subject in his italian realm ; and , next , to commence by an 
 excursion to the sea of the Baltic the sentence of banishment which I 
 have no doubt will accompany the same act that restore to the chief of 
 your House his land and his honour . " 

 the count start . 

 " that restoration , " say the austrian prince , who have advance to 
 Harley 's side , " I already guarantee . Disgrace that you be , Giulio 
 Franzini , to the noble of the Empire , I will not leave my royal master 
 till his hand strike your name from the roll . I have here your own 
 letter , to prove that your kinsman be dupe by yourself into the 
 revolt which you would have head as a catiline , if it have not well 
 suit your nature to betray it as a Judas . in ten day from this time , 
 these letter will be lay before the emperor and his Council . " 

 " be you satisfied , Monsieur le Comte , " say Harley , " with your 
 atonement so far ? if not , I have procure you the occasion to render it

 know now , that though , for a while , you ruin his fortune , you fail 
 to sully his hearth . his heart can grant you pardon , and hereafter his 
 hand may give you alm . Kneel then , Giulio Franzini , kneel at the foot 
 of Alphonso , Duke of Serrano . " 

 the above dialogue have be in French , which only a few of the Italians 
 present understood , and that imperfectly ; but at the name with which 
 Harley conclude his address to the count , a simultaneous cry from those 
 Italians break forth . 

 " Alphonso the Good ! Alphonso the Good ! Viva , viva , the good Duke of 
 Serrano ! " 

 and , forgetful even of the count , they crowd round the tall form of 
 Riccabocca , strive who should first kiss his hand , the very hem of his 
 garment . 

 Riccabocca 's eye overflow . the gaunt exile seem transfigure into 
 another and more kingly man . an inexpressible dignity invest he . he

 banishment and penury . 

 " thank , thank , " he continue ; " thank ! some day or other , you will all 
 perhaps return with I to the beloved land ! " 

 the austrian prince bow his head , as if in assent to the prayer . 

 " Giulio Franzini , " say the Duke of Serrano , - for so we may now call the 
 threadbare recluse of the Casino , - " have this last villanous design of 
 yours be allow by Providence , think you that there be one spot on 
 earth on which the ravisher could have be save from a father 's arm ? 
 but now , Heaven have be more kind . in this hour let I imitate its 
 mercy ; " and with relax brow the duke mildly draw near to his guilty

 from the moment the austrian prince have address he , the count have 
 preserve a profound silence , show neither repentance nor shame . 
 gather himself up , he have stand firm , glare round he like one at 
 bay . but as the duke now approach , he wave his hand , and exclaim , 
 " back , pedant ; back ; you have not triumph yet . and you , prate 
 German , tell your tale to our emperor . I shall be by his throne to 
 answer , - if , indeed , you escape from the meeting to which I will force 
 you by the way . " he speak , and make a rush towards the side of the 
 vessel . but Harley 's quick wit have foresee the count 's intention , and 
 Harley 's quick eye have give the signal by which it be frustrate . 
 seize in the gripe of his own watchful and indignant countryman , just

 pinion clown . then the expression of his whole countenance change ;

 strength enable he to break loose more than once , to dash more than

 though still struggle , all dignity , all attempt at presence of mind 
 gone , uttering curse the most plebeian , gnash his tooth , and foaming

 coarse fury of the fierce natural man . 

 then still preserve that air and tone of exquisite imperturbable 
 irony , which the high comedian might have seek to imitate in vain , 
 Harley bow low to the storm count . 

 " Adieu , Monsieur le Comte , adieu ! the vessel which you have honour I 
 by enter be bind to Norway . the Italians who accompany you be 
 send by yourself into exile , and , in return , they now kindly promise to 
 enliven you with their society , whenever you feel somewhat tired of 
 your own . conduct the count to his cabin . gently there , gently . Adieu , 
 Monsieur le Comte , adieu ! et bon voyage . " 

 Harley turn lightly on his heel , as Peschiera , in spite of his 
 struggle , be now fairly carry down to the cabin . 

 " a trick for the trickster , " say L'Estrange to the austrian prince . 
 " the revenge of a farce on the would - be tragedian . " 

 " More than that,-he be ruin . " 

 " and ridiculous , " quoth Harley . " I should like to see his look when 
 they land he in Norway . " Harley then pass towards the centre of the 
 vessel , by which , hitherto partially conceal by the sailor , who be 
 now busily occupy , stand Beatrice , - Frank Hazeldean , who have first 
 receive she on enter the vessel , stand by her side ; and Leonard , 
 a little apart from the two , in quiet observation of all that have pass 
 around he . Beatrice appear but little to heed Frank ; her dark eye 
 be lift to the dim starry sky , and her lip be move as if in 
 prayer ; yet her young lover be speak to she in great emotion , low 
 and rapidly . 

 " no , no , do not think for a moment that we suspect you , Beatrice . I will 
 answer for your honour with my life . oh , why will you turn from I ; why 
 will you not speak ? " 

 " a moment later , " say Beatrice , softly . " give I one moment yet . " she 
 pass slowly and falteringly towards Leonard , place her hand , that 
 tremble , on his arm , and lead he aside to the verge of the vessel . 
 Frank , startle by her movement , make a step as if to follow , and

 it be but a few word that Beatrice speak , it be but a sentence or so

 " my Lord , " she say , very firmly , " I can not accuse you of harshness 
 to my sinful and unhappy brother . his offence might perhaps deserve a 
 heavy punishment than that which you inflict with such playful scorn . 
 but whatever his penance , contempt now or poverty later , I feel that 
 his sister should be by his side to share it . I be not innocent if he be

 vessel . all that I entreat of you be , to order your man to respect my 
 brother , since a woman will be by his side . " 

 " but , Marchesa , this can not be ; and - " 

 " Beatrice , Beatrice - and I ! - our betrothal ? do you forget I ? " cry 
 Frank , in reproachful agony . 

 " no , young and too noble lover ; I shall remember you ever in my prayer . 
 but listen . I have be deceive , hurry on , I might say , by other , 
 but also , and far more , by my own mad and blinded heart , - deceive , 
 hurry on , to wrong you and to belie myself . my shame burn into I 
 when I think that I could have inflict on you the just anger of your 
 family , link you to my own ruin fortune , - my own - " 

 " your own generous , love heart ! - that be all I ask ! " cry Frank . 

 " cease , cease ! that heart be mine still ! " tear gush from the 
 Italian 's eye . 

 " Englishman , I never love you ; this heart be dead to you , and it will 
 be dead to all else forever . farewell . you will forget I soon than 
 you think for , - soon than I shall forget you , as a friend , as a 
 brother - if brother have nature as tender and as kind as your ! now , my 
 Lord , will you give I your arm ? I would join the count . " 

 " stay ; one word , Madame , " say Frank , very pale , and through his set 
 tooth , but calmly , and with a pride on his brow which have never before 
 dignify its habitual careless expression , - " one word . I may not be 
 worthy of you in anything else , but an honest love , that never doubt , 
 never suspect , that would have clung to you though all the world be 
 against , - such a love make the mean man of worth . one word , frank 
 and open . by all that you hold most sacred in your creed , do you speak 
 the truth when you say that you never love I ? " 

 Beatrice bend down her head ; she be abash before this manly nature 
 that she have so deceive , and perhaps till then undervalue . 

 " Pardon , pardon , " she say , in reluctant accent , half - choke by the 
 rise of a sob . 

 at her hesitation , Frank 's face light as if with sudden hope . she 
 raise her eye , and see the change in he , then glance where Leonard 
 stand , mournful and motionless . she shiver , and add firmly , 

 " yes , pardon ; for I speak the truth , and I have no heart to give . it 
 might have be as wax to another , - it be of granite to you . " she 
 pause , and mutter inly , " Granite , and - break ! " 

 Frank say not a word more . he stand rooted to the spot , not even gaze 
 after Beatrice as she pass on , lean on the arm of Lord L'Estrange . 
 he then walk resolutely away , and watch the boat that the man be 
 now lower from the side of the vessel . Beatrice stop when she come

 father 's anxious question . as she stop , she lean more heavily upon 
 Harley . " it be your arm that tremble now , Lord L'Estrange , " say she , 
 with a mournful smile , and , quit he ere he could answer , she bow 
 down her head meekly before Violante . " you have pardon I already , " 
 she say , in a tone that reach only the girl 's ear , " and my last word 
 shall not be of the past . I see your future spread bright before I 
 under those steadfast star . love still ; hope and trust . these be the 
 last word of she who will soon die to the world . fair maid , they be 
 prophetic ! " 

 Violante shrink back to her father 's breast , and there hide her glowing 
 face , resign her hand to Beatrice , who press it to her bosom . 
 the marchesa then come back to Harley , and disappear with he in the 
 interior of the vessel . 

 when Harley again come on deck , he seem much flurried and disturb . 
 he keep aloof from the duke and Violante , and be the last to enter the 
 boat , that be now lower into the water . 

 as he and his companion reach the land , they see the vessel in 
 movement , glide slowly down the river . " Courage , Leonard , courage ! " 
 murmur Harley . " you grieve , and nobly . but you have shun the bad 
 and most vulgar deceit in civilized life ; you have not simulate love . 
 well that yon poor lady should be , awhile , the sufferer from a harsh 
 truth , than the eternal martyr of a flattering lie ! alas , my Leonard ! 
 with the love of the poet 's dream be link only the Graces ; with the 
 love of the human heart come the awful fate ! " 

 " my Lord , poet do not dream when they love . you will learn how the 
 feeling be deep in proportion as the fancy be vivid , when you read 
 that confession of genius and woe which I have leave in your hand . " 

 Leonard turn away . Harley 's gaze follow he with inquire interest , 
 and suddenly encounter the soft dark grateful eye of Violante . " the 
 Fates , the Fates ! " murmur Harley . 




 CHAPTER IX . 

 we be at Norwood in the sage 's drawing - room . Violante have long since 
 retire to rest . Harley , who have accompany the father and daughter to 
 their home , be still converse with the former . 

 " indeed , my dear Duke , " say Harley 

 " hush , hush ! diavolo , do not   call I Duke yet ; I be at home here once 
 more as Dr. Riccabocca . " 

 " my dear doctor , then , allow I to assure you that you overrate my claim 
 to your thank . your old friend , Leonard and Frank Hazeldean , must come 
 in for their share . nor be the faithful Giacomo to be forget . " 

 " continue your explanation . " 

 " in the first place , I learn , through Frank , that one Baron Levy , a 
 certain fashionable money - lender , and general ministrant to the 
 affair of fine gentleman , be just about to purchase a yacht from Lord 
 Spendquick on behalf of the count . a short interview with Spendquick 
 enable I to outbid the usurer , and conclude a bargain by which the 
 yacht become mine , - a promise to assist Spendquick in extricate 
 himself from the claw of the money - lender   which I trust to do by 
 reconcile he with his father , who be a man of liberality and sense 
 make Spendquick readily connive at my scheme for outwit the enemy . 
 he allow Levy to suppose that the count might take possession of 
 the vessel ; but affect an engagement , and stand out for term , 
 postpone the final settlement of the purchase - money till the next day . 
 I be thus master of the vessel , which I feel sure be destine to serve 
 Peschiera 's infamous design . but it be my business not to alarm the 
 count 's suspicion ; I therefore permit the pirate crew he have 
 get together to come on board . I know I could get rid of they when 
 necessary . meanwhile , Frank undertake to keep close to the count until 
 he could see and cage within his lodging the servant whom Peschiera 
 have commission to attend his sister . if I could but apprehend this 
 servant , I have a sanguine hope that I could discover and free your 
 daughter before Peschiera could even profane she with his presence . but 
 Frank , alas ! be no pupil of Machiavelli . perhaps the count detect his 
 secret thought under his open countenance , perhaps merely wish to 
 get rid of a companion very much in his way ; but , at all event , he 
 contrive to elude our young friend as cleverly as you or I could have 
 do , - tell he that Beatrice herself be at Roehampton , have borrow 
 the count 's carriage to go there , volunteer to take Frank to the 
 house , take he . Frank find himself in a drawing - room ; and after 
 wait a few minute , while the count go out on pretence of see 
 his sister , in pirouette a certain distinguished opera - dancer ! 
 meanwhile the count be fast back on the road to London , and Frank have 
 to return as he could . he then hunt for the count everywhere , and see 
 he no more . it be late in the day when Frank find I out with this 
 news . I become seriously alarmed . Peschiera might perhaps learn my 
 counter - scheme with the yacht , or he might postpone sailing until he have 
 terrify or entangle Violante into some - in short , everything be to 
 be dread from a man of the count 's temper . I have no clew to the place 
 to which your daughter be take , no excuse to arrest Peschiera , no 
 mean even of learn where he be . he have not return to Mivart 's . 
 the Police be at fault , and useless , except in one valuable piece

 Peschiera 's perfidy have send into exile , be to be find . I 
 commission Giacomo to seek these man out , and induce they to man the 
 vessel . it might be necessary , should Peschiera or his confidential 
 servant come aboard , after we have expel or draw off the pirate 
 crew , that they should find Italians whom they might well mistake for 
 their own hireling . to these foreigner I add some english sailor 
 who have before serve in the same vessel , and on whom Spendquick assure 
 I I could rely . still these precaution only avail in case Peschiera 
 should resolve to sail , and defer till then all machination against 
 his captive . while , amidst my fear and uncertainty , I be struggle 
 still to preserve presence of mind , and rapidly discuss with the 
 austrian prince if any other step could be take , or if our sole 
 resource be to repair to the vessel and take the chance of what might 
 ensue , Leonard suddenly and quietly enter my room . you know his 
 countenance , in which joy or sadness be not betray so much by 
 the evidence of the passion as by variation in the intellectual 
 expression . it be but by the clear brow and the steady eye that I 
 see he have good tiding to impart . " 

 " ah , " say Riccabocca , - for so , obey his own request , we will yet 
 call the sage , - " ah , I early teach that young man the great lesson 
 inculcate by Helvetius . ' all our error arise from our ignorance or our 
 passion . ' without ignorance and without passion , we should be serene , 
 all - penetrate intelligence . " 

 " mopstick , " quoth Harley , " have neither ignorance nor passion ; but as 
 for their intelligence - " 

 " Pshaw ! " interrupt Riccabocca , - " proceed . " 

 " Leonard have part from we some hour before . I have commission he 
 to call at Madame di Negra 's , and , as he be familiarly know to her 
 servant , seek to obtain quietly all the information he could collect , 
 and , at all event , procure   what in my haste I have fail to do   the 
 name and description of the man who have drive she out in the morning , 
 and make what use he judge good of every hint he could gather or glean 
 that might aid our research . Leonard only succeed in learn the 
 name and description of the coachman , whom he recognize as one Beppo , 
 to whom she have often give order in his presence . none could say where 
 he then could be find , if not at the count 's hotel . Leonard go next 
 to that hotel . the man have not be there all the day . while revolve 
 what next he should do , his eye catch sight of your intended 
 son - in - law , glide across the opposite side of the street . one of those 
 luminous , inspire conjecture , which never occur to you philosopher , 
 have from the first guide Leonard to believe that Randal Leslie be 
 mix up in this villanous affair . " 

 " ha ! he ? " cry Riccabocca . " impossible ! for what interest , what 
 object ? " 

 " I can not tell , neither could leonard ; but we have both form the same 
 conjecture . brief : Leonard resolve to follow randal Leslie , and 
 track all his movement . he do then follow he , unobserved , - and at a 
 distance , first to Audley Egerton 's house , then to Eaton Square , thence 
 to a house in Bruton Street , which Leonard ascertain to be Baron 
 Levy 's . suspicious that , my clear sage ? " 

 " Diavolo , yes ! " say Riccabocca , thoughtfully . 

 " at Levy 's , Randal stay till dusk . he then come out , with his 
 cat - like , stealthy step , and walk quickly into the neighbourhood of 
 Leicester Square . Leonard see he enter one of those small hotel which 
 be appropriate to foreigner . wild , outlandish fellow be loiter 
 about the door and in the street . Leonard divine that the count or the 
 count 's confidant be there . " 

 " if that can be prove , " cry Riccabocca , " if Randal could have be 
 thus in communication with Peschiera , could have connive at such 
 perfidy , I be release from my promise . oh , to prove it ! " 

 " Proof will come later , if we be on the right track . let I go on . 
 while wait near the door of this hotel , Beppo himself , the very man 
 Leonard be in search of , come forth , and , after speak a few word 
 to some of the loitering foreigner , walk briskly towards Piccadilly . 
 Leonard here resign all further heed of Leslie , and give chase 
 to Beppo , whom he recognize at a glance . come up to he , he say 
 quietly , ' I have a letter for the Marchesa di Negra . she tell I I be 
 to send it to she by you . I have be search for you the whole day . ' 
 the man fall into the trap , and the more easily , because - as he since 
 own in excuse for a simplicity which , I dare say , weigh on his

 commit in the course of his illustrious life - he have be employ 
 by the marchesa as a spy upon Leonard , and , with an Italian 's acuman in 
 affair of the heart , detect her secret . " 

 " what secret ? " ask the innocent sage . 

 " her love for the handsome young poet . I betray that secret , in order to 
 give she some slight excuse for become Peschiera 's tool . she believe

 treason . Violante , no doubt , will explain this to you . well , the man 
 fall into the trap . ' give I the letter , Signor , and quick . ' 

 " ' it be at a hotel close by ; come there , and you will have a guinea for

 " so Leonard walk our gentleman into my hotel ; and have take he

 austrian magnate , his countenance change , and his courage fall . what

 Peschiera be to go to the house at midnight , thence to transport she to 
 the vessel . Beppo have receive order to bring the carriage to Leicester

 by the help of the police , force an entrance , and rescue your daughter ? 
 this be a very hazardous resource . the abode , which , at various time , 
 have serve for the hiding - place of man haunt by the law , abound , 
 accord to our informant , in subterranean vault and secret passage , 
 and have more than one outlet on the river . at our first summon at the 
 door , therefore , the ruffian within might not only escape themselves ,

 have betray and might still desert she - how contemplate that fearful

 flash on I : Beppo be to conduct the count to the house ; if I could 
 accompany Beppo in disguise , enter the house , myself be present ? - I 
 rush back to our informant , now become our agent ; I find the plan 
 still more feasible than I have at first suppose . Beppo have ask the 
 count 's permission to bring with he a brother accustom to the sea , 
 and who wish to quit England . I might personate that brother . you 
 know that the italian language , in most of its dialect and variety of 
 patois - Genoese , Piedmontese , Venetian - be as familiar tome as Addison 's 
 English ! alas ! rather more so . Presto ! the thing be settle . I feel my 
 heart , from that moment , as light as a feather , and my sense as keen as

 and explain in a sentence . it be right that you should be present 
 on board the vessel , not only to witness your foe 's downfall , but to

 want , till on board . 

 " Frank , accompany by Beppo   for there be yet time for these 
 preparation before midnight , repair to the yacht , take Giacomo by

 and sanction by the presence of Frank , as the count 's friend and 
 prospective brother - in - law , tell Peschiera 's hireling that they be to 
 quit the vessel , and wait on shore under Giacomo 's auspex till further 
 order ; and as soon as the deck be clear of these ruffian   save a

 of grog , your inestimable servant quietly ship on board the Italians 
 press into the service , and Frank take charge of the english sailor . 

 " the prince , promise to be on board in due time , then leave I to make 
 arrangement for his journey to Vienna by the dawn . I hasten to a 
 masquerade warehouse , where , with the help of an ingenious stagewright 
 artificer , I disguise myself into a most thorough - pace - look 
 cut - throat , and then wait the return of my friend Beppo with the most 
 perfect confidence . " 

 " yet , if that rascal have play false , all these precaution be lose . 
 Cospetto ! you be not wise , " say the prudent philosopher . 

 " very likely not . you would have be so wise , that by this time your 
 daughter would have be lose to you forever . " 

 " but why not employ the police ? " 

 " first , because I have already employ they to little purpose ; secondly , 
 because I no long want they ; thirdly , because to use they for 
 my final catastrophe would be to drag your name , and your daughter 's 
 perhaps , before a police court , - at all event , before the tribunal 
 of public gossip ; and lastly , because , have decide upon the proper 
 punishment , it have too much of equity to be quite consistent with law ; 
 and in forcibly seize a man 's person , and ship he off to Norway , 
 my police would have be sadly in the way . certainly my plan rather 
 savour of Lope de Vega than of Blackstone . however , you see success 
 atone for all irregularity . I resume : Beppo come back in time to

 undertake to manage the boat . 

 " I be nearly at the close of my story . sure of my disguise , I get on the

 handsome race your ! drive on . ' 

 " we arrive at the house . I dismount to open the carriage - door . the 
 count give I one look . ' Beppo say you have know the sea . ' 

 " ' Excellency , yes . I be a Genoese . ' 

 " ' ha ! how be that ? Beppo be a Lombard . ' - admire the readiness with which 
 I redeem my blunder . 

 " ' Excellency , it please Heaven that Beppo should be bear in Lombardy , 
 and then to remove my respected parent to Genoa , at which city they 
 be so kindly treat that my mother , in common gratitude , be bind 
 to increase its population . it be all she could do , poor woman . you see 
 she do she good . ' 

 " the count smile , and say no more . the door open , I follow he ; 
 your daughter can tell you the rest . " 

 " and you risk your life in that den of miscreant ! noble friend ! " 

 " risk my life , - no ; but I risk the count 's . there be one moment 
 when my hand be on my trigger , and my soul very near the sin of 
 justifiable homicide . but my tale be do . the count be now on the

 aloof from shore , and they will then land the count and the marchesa , by

 " would he have that audacity ? " 

 " do he more justice ! audacity , faith ! he do not want for that . but I 
 dread not his appearance at Vienna with such evidence against he . 
 I dread his encounter the prince on the road , and force a duel , 
 before his character be so blast that the prince could refuse it ; and 
 the count be a dead shot of course , - all such man be ! " 

 " he will return , and you - " 

 " I ! oh , never fear ; he have have enough of I . and now , my dear 
 friend , - now that Violante be safe once more under your own roof ;

 Leonard , who leave we to go to she , that our success have be achieve

 my eye be heavy , and if you stay here three minute long , I shall be 
 out of reach of hearing , in the land of dream . Buona notte ! " 

 " but there be a bed prepare for you . " 

 Harley shake his head in dissent , and compose himself at length on the 
 sofa . 

 Riccabocca , bending , wrap the cloak round his guest , kiss he on 
 the forehead , and creep out of the room to rejoin Jemima , who still sit

 Riccabocca here laugh merrily , and his wife throw herself on his 
 shoulder , and cry for joy . 

 but no sleep fall on the lid of Harley L'Estrange . he start up when

 Italy , and marry he there , in the hope of improve the breed of our

 " ah , " say Harley , " you will soon leave our cold shore . may sunshine

 CHAPTER X. 

 Harley have not long reach his hotel , and be still seat before his 
 untasted breakfast , when Mr. Randal Leslie be announce . Randal , who 
 be in the firm belief that Violante be now on the wide sea with 
 Peschiera , enter , look the very personation of anxiety and fatigue . 
 for like the great Cardinal Richelieu , Randal have learn the art how 
 to make good use of his own delicate and somewhat sickly aspect . the 
 cardinal , when intent on some sanguinary scheme require unusual 
 vitality and vigour , contrive to make himself look a harmless sufferer 
 at death 's door . and Randal , whose nervous energy could at that moment 
 have whirl he from one end of this huge metropolis to the other , with 
 a speed that would have outstrip a prize pedestrian , now sink into 
 a chair with a jaded weariness that no mother could have see without 
 compassion . he seem since the last night to have gallop towards the 
 last stage of consumption . 

 " have you discover no trace , my Lord ? speak , speak ! " 

 " Speak ! certainly . I be too happy to relieve your mind , Mr. Leslie . what 
 fool we be ! ha , ha ! " 

 " Fools - how ? " falter Randal . 

 " of course ; the young lady be at her father 's house all the time . " 

 " eh ? what ? " 

 " and be there now . " 

 " it be not possible ! " say Randal , in the hollow , dreamy tone of a 
 somnambulist . " at her father 's house , at Norwood ! be you sure ? " 

 " sure . " 

 Randal make a desperate and successful effort at self - control . " Heaven 
 be praise ! " he cry . " and just as I have begin to suspect the count , 
 the marchesa ; for I find that neither of they sleep at home last night ; 
 and Levy tell I that the count have write to he , request the 
 baron to discharge his bill , as he should be for some time absent from 
 England . " 

 " indeed ! well , that be nothing to we , - very much to Baron Levy , if he 
 execute his commission , and discharge the bill . what ! be you go 
 already ? " 

 " do you ask such a question ? how can I stay ? I must go to Norwood , - must 
 see Violante with my own eye ! forgive my emotion - I - I - " 

 Randal snatch at his hat and hurry away . the low scornful laugh of 
 Harley follow he as he go . 

 " I have no more doubt of his guilt than Leonard have . Violante at 
 least shall not be the prize of that thin - lipped knave . what strange 
 fascination can he possess , that he should thus bind to he the two 
 man I value most , - Audley Egerton and Alphonso di Serrano ? both so wise 
 too ! - one in book , one in action . and both suspicious man ! while I , so 
 imprudently trustful and frank - ah , that be the reason ; our nature be 
 antipathetic ; cunning , simulation , falsehood , I have no mercy , no pardon 
 for these . woe to all hypocrite if I be a grand inquisitor ! " 

 " Mr. Richard Avenel , " say the waiter , throw open the door . 

 Harley catch at the arm of the chair on which he sit , and grasp 
 it nervously , while his eye become fix intently on the form of the 
 gentleman who now advance into the room . he rise with an effort . 

 " Mr. Avenel ! " he say falteringly . " do I hear your name aright ? 
 Avenel ! " 

 " Richard Avenel , at your service , my Lord , " answer Dick . " my family be 
 not unknown to you ; and I be not ashamed of my family , though my parent 
 be small Lansmere tradesfolk , and I be - ahem ! - a citizen of the 
 world , and well - to - do ! " add Dick , drop his kid glove into his 
 hat , and then place the hat on the table , with the air of an old 
 acquaintance who wish to make himself at home . Lord L'Estrange bow 
 and say , as he reseat himself   Dick be firmly seat already , 
 " you be most welcome , sir ; and if there be anything I can do for one of 
 your name - " 

 " thank you , my Lord , " interrupt Dick . " I want nothing of any man . a 
 bold word to say ; but I say it . nevertheless , I should not have presume 
 to call on your lordship , unless , indeed , you have do I the honour 
 to call first at my house , Eaton Square , no . - - I should not have 
 presume to call if it have not be on business , - public business , I may 
 say - national business ! " 

 Harley bow again . a faint smile flit for a moment to his lip , but , 
 vanishing , give way to a mournful , absent expression of countenance , as 
 he scan the handsome feature before he , and , perhaps , masculine and 
 bold though they be , still discover something of a family likeness 
 to one whose beauty have once be his ideal of female loveliness ; for 
 suddenly he stretch forth his hand , and say , with more than his usual 
 cordial sweetness , " business or not business , let we speak to each other 
 as friend , - for the sake of a name that take I back to Lansmere , to 
 my youth . I listen to you with interest . " 

 Richard Avenel , much surprised by this unexpected kindliness , and 
 touch , he know not why , by the soft and melancholy tone of Harley 's 
 voice , warmly press the hand hold out to he ; and seize with a rare 
 fit of shyness , colour and coughed and hem and look first down , 
 then aside , before he could find the word which be generally ready 
 enough at his command . 

 " you be very good , Lord L'Estrange ; nothing can be handsomer . I feel it 
 here , my Lord , " strike his buff waistcoat , - " I do , ' pon my honour . but 
 not to waste your time   time 's money , I come to the point . it be about 
 the borough of Lansmere . your family interest be very strong in that 
 borough ; but excuse I if I say that I do not   think you be aware that I 
 too have cook up a pretty considerable interest on the other side . no 
 offence , - opinion be free . and the popular tide run strong with we - I 
 mean with I - at the impending crisis , - that be , at the next election . 
 now , I have a great respect for the earl your father , and so have those 
 who bring I into the world - my father , John , be always a regular 
 good Blue , - and my respect for yourself since I come into this room 
 have go up in the market a very great rise indeed , - considerable . so I 
 should just like to see if we could set our head together , and settle 
 the borough between we two , in a snug private way , as public man ought 
 to do when they get together , nobody else by , and no necessity for that 
 sort of humbug , which be so common in this rotten old country . eh , my 
 Lord ? " 

 " Mr. Avenel , " say Harley , slowly , recover himself from the 
 abstraction with which he have listen to Dick 's early sentence , " I 
 fear I do not quite understand you ; but I have no other interest in the 
 next election for the borough of Lansmere than as may serve one whom , 
 whatever be your politic , you must acknowledge to be - " 

 " a humbug ! " 

 " Mr. Avenel , you can not mean the person I mean . I speak of one of the 
 first statesman of our time , - of Mr. Audley Egerton , of - " 

 " a stiff - neck , pompous - " 

 " my early and dear friend . " 

 the rebuke , though gently say , suffice to silence Dick for a moment ; 
 and when he speak again , it be in an altered tone . 

 " I beg your pardon , my Lord , I be sure . of course , I can say nothing 
 disrespectful of your friend , - very sorry that he be your friend . in 
 that case , I be almost afraid that nothing be to be do . but Mr. Audley 
 Egerton have not a chance . 

 " let I convince you of this . " and Dick pull out a little book , bind 
 neatly in red . 

 " Canvass book , my Lord . I be no aristocrat . I do not   pretend to carry a 
 free and independent constituency in my breech ' pocket . heaven forbid ! 
 but as a practical man of business , what I do be do properly . just 
 look at this book . 

 " well kept , eh ? name , promise , inclination , public opinion , and 
 private interest of every individual Lansmere elector ! now , as one man 
 of honour to another , I show you this book , and I think you will see 
 that we have a clear majority of at least eighty vote as against Mr. 
 Egerton . " 

 " that be your view of the question , " say Harley , take the book 
 and glance over the name catalogue and ticket therein . but his 
 countenance become serious as he recognize many name familiar to his 
 boyhood as those of important elector on the Lansmere side , and which 
 he now find transfer to the hostile . " but surely there be person 
 here in whom you deceive yourself , - old friend of my family , stanch 
 supporter of our party . " 

 " exactly so . but this new question have turn all old thing 
 topsy - turvy . no rely on any friend of your . no reliance except in 
 this book ! " say Dick , slap the red cover with calm but ominous 
 emphasis . 

 " now , what I want to propose be this : do not   let the Lansmere interest be 
 beat ; it would vex the old earl , - go to his heart , I be sure . " 

 Harley nod . 

 " and the Lansmere interest need not be beat , if you will put up another 
 man instead of this red - tapist .   Beg pardon .   you see I only want to get 
 in one man , you want to get in another . why not ? now , there be a smart 
 youth , - connection of Mr. Egerton 's , - randal Leslie . I have no objection 
 to he , though he be of your colour . withdraw Mr. Egerton , and I will 
 withdraw my second man before it come to the poll ; and so we shall 
 halve the borough slick between we . that be the way to do business , - eh , 
 my Lord ? " 

 " randal Leslie ! oh , you wish to bring in Mr. Leslie ? but he stand with 
 Egerton , not against he . " 

 " ah , " say Dick , smile as if to himself , ' so I hear ; and we could 
 bring he in over Egerton without say a word to you . but all our 
 family respect your , and so I have wish to do the thing handsome and 
 open . let the earl and your party be content with young Leslie . " 

 " Young Leslie have speak to you ? " 

 " not as to my come here . oh , no , that be a secret , - private and 
 confidential , my Lord . and now , to make matter still more smooth , I 
 propose that my man shall be one to your Lordship 's own heart . I find 
 you have be very kind to my nephew ; do you credit , my Lord , - a 
 wonderful young man , though I say it . I never guess there be so much 
 in he . yet all the time he be in my house , he have in his desk the very 
 sketch of an invention that be now save I from ruin , - from positive 
 ruin , - Baron Levy , the King 's Bench , and almighty smash ! now , such 
 a young man ought to be in parliament . I like to bring forward a 
 relation , - that be , when he do one credit ; ' t be human nature and 
 sacred tie - one 's own flesh and blood ; and besides , one hand rub the 
 other , and one leg help on the other , and relation get on well in 
 the world when they pull together ; that be , suppose that they be the 
 proper sort of relation , and pull one on , not down . I have once think 
 of stand for Lansmere myself , - think of it very lately . the country 
 want man like I , I know that ; but I have an idea that I have well see 
 to my own business . the country may , or may not , do without I , stupid 
 old thing that she be ! but my mill and my new engine - there be no doubt 
 that they can not do without I . in short , as we be quite alone , and , 
 as I say before , there be no kind of necessity for that sort of humbug 
 which exist when other people be present , provide elsewhere for Mr. 
 Egerton , whom I hate like poison , - I have a right to do that , I suppose , 
 without offence to your Lordship , - and the two younker , Leonard 
 Fairfield and Randal Leslie , shall be member for the free and 
 independent borough of Lansmere ! " 

 " but do Leonard wish to come into parliament ? " 

 " no , he say not ; but that be nonsense . if your Lordship will just 
 signify your wish that he should not lose this noble opportunity to 
 raise himself in life , and get something handsome out of the nation , I be 
 sure he owe you too much to hesitate , - ' specially when ' t be to his own 
 advantage . and besides , one of we Avenels ought to be in parliament ; and 
 if I have not the time and learning , and so forth , and he have , why , it 
 stand to reason that he should be the man . and if he can do something 
 for I one day - not that I want anything - but still a baronetcy or so 
 would be a compliment to British Industry , and be appreciate as such by 
 myself and the public at large , - I say , if he could do something of 
 that sort , it would keep up the whole family ; and if he can not , why , I will 
 forgive he . " 

 " Avenel , " say Harley , with that familiar and gracious charm of manner 
 which few ever could resist , " Avenel , if as a great personal favour to 
 myself - to I your fellow - townsman   I be bear at Lansmere   - if I ask 
 you to forego your grudge against Audley Egerton , whatever that grudge 
 be , and not oppose his election , while our party would not oppose your 
 nephew 's , could you not oblige I ? come , for the sake of dear Lansmere , 
 and all the old kindly feeling between your family and mine , say ' yes , 
 so shall it be . ' " 

 Richard Avenel be almost melt . he turn away his face ; but there 
 suddenly rise to his recollection the scornful brow of Audley Egerton , 
 the lofty contempt with which he , then the worshipful Mayor of 
 Screwstown , have be show out of the minister 's office - room ; and the 
 blood rush over his cheek , he stamp his foot on the floor , and 
 exclaim angrily , " no ; I swear that Audley Egerton should smart for his 
 insolence to I , as sure as my name be Richard Avenel ; and all the soft 
 soap in the world will not wash out that oath . so there be nothing for 
 it but for you to withdraw that man , or for I to defeat he . and I 
 would do so , ay , - and in the way that could most gall he , - if it cost 
 I half my fortune . but it will not cost that , " say Dick , cooling , " nor 
 anything like it ; for when the popular tide run in one 's favour , ' t 
 be astonish how cheap an election may be . it will cost he enough 
 though , and all for nothing , - bad than nothing . think of it , my Lord . " 

 " I will , Mr. Avenel . and I say , in my turn , that my friendship be as 
 strong as your hate ; and that if it cost I , not half , but my whole 
 fortune , Audley Egerton shall come in without a shilling of expense to 
 himself , should we once decide that he stand the contest . " 

 " very well , my Lord , - very well , " say Dick , stiffly , and draw on 
 his kid glove ; " we will see if the aristocracy be always to ride over 
 the free choice of the people in this way . but the people be rouse , 
 my Lord . the March of Enlightenment be commence , the Schoolmaster be 
 abroad , and the British Lion - " 

 " nobody here but ourselves , my dear avenel . be not this rather what you 
 call - humbug ? " 

 Dick start , star , colour , and then burst out laugh , " give we 
 your hand again , my Lord . you be a good fellow , that you be . and for 
 your sake - " 

 " you will not oppose Egerton ? " 

 " tooth and nail , tooth and nail ! " cry Dick , clap his hand to his 
 ear , and fairly run out of the room . 

 there pass over Harley 's countenance that change so frequent to 
 it , - more frequent , indeed , to the gay child of the world than those 
 of consistent temper and uniform habit might suppose . there be many a 
 man whom we call friend , and whose face seem familiar to we as our own ; 
 yet , could we but take a glimpse of he when we leave his presence , and 
 he sink back into his chair alone , we should sigh to see how often the 
 smile on the frank lip be but a bravery of the drill , only wear when 
 on parade . 

 what thought do the visit of Richard Avenel bequeath to Harley ? it 
 be hard to define they . 

 in his place , an Audley Egerton would have take some comfort from the 
 visit , would have murmur , " thank Heaven ! I have not to present to the 
 world that terrible man as my brother - in - law . " but probably Harley have 
 escape , in his revery , from Richard Avenel altogether . even as the 
 slight incident in the daytime cause our dream at night , but be 
 itself clean forget , so the name , so the look of the visitor , might 
 have suffice but to influence a vision , as remote from its casual 
 suggester as what we call real life be from that life much more real , 
 that we imagine , or remember , in the haunted chamber of the brain . for 
 what be real life ? how little the thing actually do around we affect 
 the spring of our sorrow or joy ; but the life which our dulness call 
 romance , - the sentiment , the remembrance , the hope , or the fear , that 
 be never see in the toil of our hand , never hear in the jargon on 
 our lip , - from that life all spin , as the spider from its entrail , 
 the web by which we hang in the sunbeam , or glide out of sight into the 
 shelter of home . 

 " I must not think , " say Harley , rouse himself with a sigh , " either of 
 past or present . let I hurry on to some fancied future . ' happy be 
 the marriage , ' say the french philosopher , and still say many a 
 sage , ' in which man ask only the mild companion , and woman but the calm 
 protector . ' I will go to Helen . " 

 he rise ; and as he be about to lock up his escritoire , he remember 
 the paper which Leonard have request he to read . he take they from 
 their deposit , with a careless hand , intend to carry they with he 
 to his father 's house . but as his eye fall upon the character , the hand 
 suddenly tremble , and he recoil some pace , as if strike by a violent 
 blow . then , gaze more intently on the writing , a low cry break from 
 his lip . he reseat himself , and begin to read . 




 CHAPTER XI . 

 randal - with many misgiving at Lord L'Estrange 's tone , in which he 
 be at no loss to detect a latent irony - proceed to Norwood . he find 
 Riccabocca exceedingly cold and distant ; but he soon bring that sage 
 to communicate the suspicion which Lord L'Estrange have instill 
 into his mind , and these Randal be as speedily enable to dispel . he 
 account at once for his visit to Levy and Peschiera . naturally he have 
 seek Levy , an acquaintance of his own , - nay , of Audley Egerton 's , - but 
 whom he know to be professionally employ by the count . he have 
 succeed in extract from the baron Peschiera 's suspicious change of 
 lodgment from Mivart 's Hotel to the purlieus of Leicester Square ; have 
 call there on the count , force an entrance , openly accuse he 
 of abstract Violante ; high word have pass between they , - even a 
 challenge . randal produce a note from a military friend of his , whom he 
 have send to the count an hour after quit the hotel . this note state 
 that arrangement be make for a meeting near Lord 's Cricket Ground , 
 at seven o'clock the next morning . randal then submit to Riccabocca 
 another formal memorandum from the same warlike friend , to the purport 
 that randal and himself have repair to the ground , and no count have 
 be forthcoming . it must be own that Randal have take all suitable 
 precaution to clear himself . such a man be not to blame for want of 
 invention , if he be sometimes doom to fail . 

 " I , then , much alarmed , " continue Randal , " hasten to Baron Levy , who 
 inform I that the count have write he word that he should be for 
 some time absent from England . rush thence , in despair , to your 
 friend Lord L'Estrange , I hear that your daughter be safe with you . 
 and though , as I have just prove , I would have risk my life against 
 so notorious a duellist as the count , on the mere chance of preserve 
 Violante from his suppose design , I be rejoice to think that she have 
 no need of my unskilful arm . but how and why can the count have leave 
 England after accept a challenge ? a man so sure of his weapon , 
 too , - repute to be as fearless of danger as he be blunt in conscience . 
 explain , - you who know mankind so well , - explain . I can not . " the 
 philosopher could not resist the pleasure of narrate the detection and 
 humiliation of his foe , the wit , ingenuity , and readiness of his 
 friend . so Randal learn , by little and little , the whole drama of the 
 precede night . he see , then , that the exile have all reasonable hope 
 of speedy restoration to rank and wealth . Violante , indeed ; would be 
 a brilliant prize , - too brilliant , perhaps , for Randal , but not to be 
 sacrifice without an effort . therefore wring convulsively the hand 
 of his meditated father - in - law , and turn away his head as if to 
 conceal his emotion , the ingenuous young suitor falter forth that 
 now Dr. Riccabocca be so soon to vanish into the Duke di Serrano , 
 he - randal Leslie of Rood , bear a gentleman , indeed , but of fall 
 fortune - have no right to claim the promise which have be give to he 
 while a father have cause to fear for a daughter 's future ; with the fear 
 cease the promise . Alight Heaven bless father and daughter both ! 

 this address touch both the heart and honour of the exile . randal 
 Leslie know his man . and though , before Randal 's visit , Riccabocca be 
 not quite so much a philosopher but what he would have be well please 
 to have find himself release , by proof of the young man 's treachery , 
 from an alliance below the rank to which he have all chance of early 
 restoration , yet no Spaniard be ever more tenacious of plighted word 
 than this inconsistent pupil of the profound florentine . and Randal 's 
 probity be now clear to he , he repeat , with stately formality , 
 his previous offer of Violante 's hand . 

 " but , " still falteringly sigh the provident and far - calculate 
 randal - " but your only child , your sole heiress ! oh , might not your 
 consent to such a marriage   if know before your recall   jeopardize your 
 cause ? your land , your principality , to devolve on the child of an 
 humble Englishman ! I dare not believe it . ah , would Violante be not 
 your heiress ! " 

 " a noble wish , " say Riccabocca , smile blandly , " and one that the 
 Fates will realize . cheer up ; Violante will not be my heiress . " 

 " ah , " cry Randal , draw a long breath - " ah , what do I hear ? " 

 " Hist ! I shall soon a second time be a father . and , to judge by the 
 unerring research of writer upon that most interesting of all 
 subject , parturitive science , I shall be the father of a son . he will , 
 of course , succeed to the title of Serrano . and Violante - " 

 " will have nothing , I suppose ? " exclaim Randal , try his good to 
 look overjoyed till he have get his paw out of the trap into which he 
 have so incautiously thrust they . 

 " Nay , her portion by our law - to say nothing of my affection - would far 
 exceed the ordinary dower which the daughter of London merchant bring 
 to the son of british peer . whoever marry Violante , provide I 
 regain my estate , must submit to the care which the poet assure we 
 ever attend on wealth . " 

 " oh ! " groan Randal , as if already bow beneath the care , and 
 sympathize with the poet . 

 " and now , let I present you to your betrothed . " although poor Randal 
 have be remorselessly hurry along what schiller call the " gamut 
 of feeling , " during the last three minute , down to the deep chord of 
 despair at the abrupt intelligence that his betrothed be no heiress 
 after all ; thence ascend to vibration of pleasant doubt as to the 
 unborn usurper of her right , accord to the prophecy of parturitive 
 science ; and lastly , swell into a concord of all sweet thought at 
 the assurance that , come what might , she would be a wealthy bride 
 than a peer 's son could discover in the matrimonial Potosi of Lombard 
 Street , - still the tormented lover be not there allow to repose 
 his exhausted though ravish soul . for , at the idea of personally 
 confront the destine bride - whose very existence have almost vanish 
 from his mind 's eye , amidst the golden shower that it see fall 
 divinely round she - Randal be suddenly remind of the exceeding 
 bluntness with which , at their last interview , it have be his policy to 
 announce his suit , and of the necessity of an impromptu falsetto suit 
 to the new variation that toss he again to and fro on the merciless 
 gamut . however , he could not recoil from her father 's proposition , 
 though , in order to prepare Riccabocca for Violante 's representation , 
 he confess pathetically that his impatience to obtain her consent and 
 baffle Peschiera have make he appear a rude and presumptuous wooer . the 
 philosopher , who be disposed to believe one kind of courtship to be 
 much the same as another , in case where the result of all courtship 
 be once predetermine , smile benignly , pat Randal 's thin cheek , 
 with a " Pooh , pooh , pazzie ! " and leave the room to summon Violante . 

 " if knowledge be power , " soliloquize Randal , " ability be certainly good 
 luck , as Miss Edgeworth show in that story of Murad the Unlucky , which 
 I read at Eton ; very clever story it be , too . so nothing come amiss to 
 I . Violante 's escape , which have cost I the count 's L10,000 , prove to 
 be worth to I , I dare say , ten time as much . no doubt she will have a 
 hundred thousand pound at the least . and then , if her father have no 
 other child , after all , or the child he expect die in infancy , why , 
 once reconcile to his government and restore to his estate , the law 
 must take its usual course , and Violante will be the great heiress 
 in Europe . as to the young lady herself , I confess she rather awe I ; I 
 know I shall be henpecke . well , all respectable husband be . there be 
 something scampish and ruffianly in not be henpecke . " here Randal 's 
 smile might have harmonize well with Pluto 's " iron tear ; " but , iron 
 as the smile be , the serious young man be ashamed of it . " what be I 
 about , " say he , half aloud , " chuckle to myself and waste time , when 
 I ought to be think gravely how to explain away my former cavalier 
 courtship ? such a masterpiece as I think it then ! but who could 
 foresee the turn thing would take ? let I think ; let I think . plague 
 on it , here she come . " 

 but Randal have not the fine ear of your more romantic lover ; and , to 
 his great relief , the exile enter the room unaccompanie by Violante . 
 Riccabocca look somewhat embarrassed . 

 " my dear Leslie , you must excuse my daughter to - day ; she be still 
 suffer from the agitation she have go through , and can not see you . " 

 the lover try not to look too delighted . 

 " Cruel ! " say he ; " yet I would not for world force myself on her 
 presence . I hope , Duke , that she will not find it too difficult to obey 
 the command which dispose of her hand , and intrust her happiness to my 
 grateful charge . " 

 " to be plain with you , Randal , she do at present seem to find it more 
 difficult than I foresaw . she even talk of - " 

 " another attachment - oh , heaven ! " 

 " Attachment , pazzie ! whom have she see ? no , a convent ! but leave it to 
 I . in a calm hour she will comprehend that a child must know no lot 
 more enviable and holy than that of redeem a father 's honour . and 
 now , if you be return to London , may I ask you to convey to young 
 Mr. Hazeldean my assurance of undye gratitude for his share in my 
 daughter 's delivery from that poor baffled swindler . " 

 it be noticeable that , now Peschiera be no long an object of dread to 
 the nervous father , he become but an object of pity to the philosopher , 
 and of contempt to the grandee . 

 " true , " say Randal , " you tell I Frank have a share in Lord L'Estrange 's 
 very clever and dramatic device . my Lord must be by nature a fine 
 actor , - comic , with a touch of melodrame ! poor Frank ! apparently he 
 have lose the woman he adore , - Beatrice di Negra . you say she have 
 accompany the count . be the marriage that be to be between she and 
 Frank break off ? " 

 " I do not know such a marriage be contemplate . I understand she to be 
 attach to another . not that that be any reason why she would not have 
 marry Mr. Hazeldean . express to he my congratulation on his escape . " 

 " Nay , he must not know that I have inadvertently betray his 
 confidence ; but you now guess , what perhaps puzzle you before , - namely , 
 how I come to be so well acquainted with the count and his movement . I 
 be so intimate with my relation Frank , and Frank be affiance to the 
 marchesa . " 

 " I be glad you give I that explanation ; it suffice . after all , the 
 marchesa be not by nature a bad woman , - that be , not bad than woman 
 generally be , so Harley say , and Violante forgive and excuse she . " 

 " Generous Violante ! but it be true . so much do the marchesa appear 
 to I possess of fine , though ill - regulate quality , that I always 
 consider she dispose to aid in frustrate her brother 's criminal 
 design . so I even say , if I remember right , to Violante . " 

 drop this prudent and precautionary sentence , in order to guard 
 against anything Violante might say as to that subtle mention of 
 Beatrice which have predispose she to confide in the marchesa , Randal 
 then hurry on , " but you want repose . I leave you the happy , the 
 most grateful of man . I will give your courteous message to Frank . " 




 CHAPTER XII . 

 curious to learn what have pass between Beatrice and Frank , and deeply 
 interested in all that could oust Frank out of the squire 's goodwill , 
 or aught that could injure his own prospect by tend to unite son and 
 father , Randal be not slow in reach his young kinsman 's lodging . it 
 might be suppose that have , in all probability , just secure so 
 great a fortune as would accompany Violante 's hand , Randal might be 
 indifferent to the success of his scheme on the Hazeldean exchequer . 
 such a supposition would grievously wrong this profound young man . 
 for , in the first place , Violante be not yet win , nor her father yet 
 restore to the estate which would defray her dower ; and , in the next 
 place , randal , like Iago , love villany for the genius it call forth 
 in he . the sole luxury the abstemious aspirer allow to himself be 
 that which be find in intellectual restlessness . untempte by wine , 
 dead to love , unamuse by pleasure , indifferent to the art , despise 
 literature save as mean to some end of power , Randal Leslie be 
 the incarnation of thought hatch out of the corruption of will . 
 at twilight we see thin airy spectral insect , all wing and nipper , 
 hovering , as if they could never pause , over some sullen mephitic pool . 
 just so , methink , hover over Acheron such gnat - like , noiseless soarer 
 into gloomy air out of stygian deep , as be the thought of spirit 
 like Randal Leslie 's . wing have they , but only the well to pounce 
 down , - draw their nutriment from unguarded material cuticle ; and just 
 when , madden , you strike , and exult exclaim , " Caught , by Jove ! " 
 wh - irr fly the diaphanous , ghostly larva , and your blow fall on your 
 own twice - offend cheek . 

 the young man who be acquaint with Randal say he have not a 
 vice ! the fact be that his whole composition be one epic vice , so 
 elaborately construct that it have not an episode which a critic could 
 call irrelevant . grand young man ! 

 " but , my dear fellow , " say Randal , as soon as he have learn from Frank 
 all that have pass on board the vessel between he and Beatrice , " I 
 can not believe this . ' never love you ' ? what be her object , then , in 
 deceive not only you , but myself ? I suspect her declaration be but 
 some heroical refinement of generosity . after her brother 's dejection 
 and probable ruin , she might feel that she be no match for you . then , 
 too , the squire 's displeasure ! I see it all ; just like she , - noble , 
 unhappy woman ! " 

 Frank shake his head . " there be moment , " say he , with a wisdom that 
 come out of those instinct which awake from the depth of youth 's 
 first great sorrow , - " moment when a woman can not feign , and there be 
 tone in the voice of a woman which man can not misinterpret . she do 
 not love I , - she never do love I ; I can see that her heart have be 
 elsewhere . no matter , - all be over . I do not   deny that I be suffer 
 an intense grief ; it gnaw like a kind of sullen hunger ; and I feel so 
 broken , too , as if I have grow old , and there be nothing leave worth 
 live for . I do not   deny all that . " 

 " my poor , dear friend , if you would but believe - " 

 " I do not   want to believe anything , except that I have be a great fool . 
 I do not   think I can ever commit such folly again . but I be a man . I 
 shall get the well of this ; I should despise myself if I could not . 
 and now let we talk of my dear father . have he leave town ? " 

 " leave last night by the mail . you can write and tell he you have give 
 up the marchesa , and all will be well again between you . " 

 " give she up ! Fie , Randal ! do you think I should tell such a lie ? she 
 give I up ; I can claim no merit out of that . " 

 " oh , yes ! I can make the squire see all to your advantage . oh , if it 
 be only the marchesa ! but , alas ! that curse postobit ! how could 
 Levy betray you ? never trust to usurer again ; they can not resist the 
 temptation of a speedy profit . 

 " they first buy the son , and then sell he to the father . and the squire 
 have such strange notion on matter of this kind . " 

 " he be right to have they . there , just read this letter from my mother . 
 it come to I this morning . I could hang myself if I be a dog ; but I be 
 a man , and so I must bear it . " 

 Randal take Mrs. Hazeldean 's letter from Frank 's tremble hand . the 
 poor mother have learn , though but imperfectly , Frank 's misdeed from 
 some hurried line which the squire have despatch to she ; and she write 
 as good , indulgent , but sensible , right - minded mother alone can write . 
 more lenient to an imprudent love than the squire , she touch with 
 discreet tenderness on Frank 's rash engagement with a foreigner , but 
 severely on his own open defiance of his father 's wish . her anger 
 be , however , reserve for that unholy post - obit . here the hearty genial 
 wife 's love overcome the mother 's affection . to count , in cold blood , on 
 that husband 's death , and to wound his heart so keenly , just where its 
 jealous , fatherly fondness make it most susceptible ! 

  " o Frank , Frank ! " write Mrs. Hazeldean , " be it not for this , be 
  it only for your unfortunate attachment to the italian lady , only 
  for your debt , only for the error of hasty , extravagant youth , I 
  should be with you now , my arm round your neck , kiss you , 
  chide you back to your father 's heart . but - but the thought that 
  between you and his heart have be the sordid calculation of his 
  death , - that be a wall between we . I can not come near you . I 
  should not like to look on your face , and think how my William 's 
  tear fall over it , when I place you , new bear , in his arm , and 
  bid he welcome his heir . what ! you a mere boy still , your father 
  yet in the prime of life , and the heir can not wait till nature 
  leave he fatherless . Frank ; Frank this be so unlike you . can 
  London have ruin already a disposition so honest and 
  affectionate ? - no ; I can not believe it . there must be some mistake . 
  clear it up , I implore you ; or , though as a mother I pity you , as a 
  wife I can not forgive . " 

 even Randal be affect by the letter ; for , as we know , even Randal 
 feel in his own person the strength of family tie . the poor squire 's 
 choler and bluffness have disguise the parental heart from an eye that , 
 however acute , have not be willing to search for it ; and randal , ever 
 affect through his intellect , have despise the very weakness on which 
 he have prey . but the mother 's letter , so just and sensible   allow 
 that the squire 's opinion have naturally influence the wife to 
 take what man of the world would call a very exaggerated view of 
 the every - day occurrence of loan raise by a son , payable only at 
 a father 's death , - this letter , I say , if exaggerate accord to 
 fashionable notion , so sensible if judge by natural affection , 
 touch the dull heart of the schemer , because approve by the quick 
 tact of his intelligence . 

 " Frank , " say he , with a sincerity that afterwards amaze himself , " go 
 down at once to Hazeldean ; see your mother , and explain to she how this 
 transaction really happen . the woman you love , and woo as wife , 
 in danger of an arrest , your distraction of mind , Levy 's counsel , your 
 hope to pay off the debt , so incur to the usurer , from the fortune 
 you would shortly receive with the marchesa . speak to your mother , - she 
 be a woman ; woman have a common interest in forgive all fault that 
 arise from the source of their power over we man , - I mean love . go ! " 

 " no , I can not go ; you see she would not like to look on my face . and I 
 can not repeat what you say so glibly . besides , somehow or other , as I be 
 so dependent upon my father , - and he have say as much , - I feel as if 
 it would be mean in I to make any excuse . I do the thing , and must 
 suffer for it . but I be a in - an - no - I ' m not a man here . " Frank burst 
 into tear . 

 at the sight of those tear , Randal gradually recover from his strange 
 aberration into vulgar and low humanity . his habitual contempt for his 
 kinsman return ; and with contempt come the natural indifference to the 
 suffering of the thing to be put to use . it be contempt for the worm
