 these preparatory oration over , a dead silence succeed , and Audley 
 Egerton arise . 

 at the first few sentence , all feel they be in the presence of one 
 accustom to command attention , and to give to opinion the weight 
 of recognize authority . the slowness of the measured accent , the 
 composure of the manly aspect , the decorum of the simple gesture , - all 
 bespoke and all become the minister of a great empire , who have less 
 agitated assembly by impassioned eloquence , than compel their 
 silent respect to the view of sagacity and experience . but what might 
 have be formal and didactic in another be relieve in Egerton by 
 that air , tone , bear of gentleman , which have a charm for the most 
 plebeian audience . he have eminently these attribute in private life ; 
 but they become far more conspicuous whenever he have to appear in 
 public . the ' senatorius decor " seem a phrase coin for he . 

 Audley commence with notice of his adversary in that language of 
 high courtesy which be so become to superior station , and which 
 augur well for victory than the most pointed diatribe of hostile 
 declamation . incline his head towards Avenel , he express regret that 
 he should be oppose by a gentleman whose birth naturally endear 
 he to the town , of which he be a distinguished native , and whose 
 honourable ambition be in itself a proof of the admirable nature 
 of that Constitution , which admit the lowly to rise to its 
 distinction , while it compel the lofty to labour and compete for 
 those honour which be the most coveted , because they be derive 
 from the trust of their countryman , and dignify by the duty which 
 the sense of responsibility entail . he pay a passing but generous

 of youth strive for place in the van of the new generation that 
 march on to replace the old , he imply that he do not consider 
 Leonard as oppose to himself , but rather as an emulous competitor for a 
 worthy prize with his " own young and value friend , Mr. Randal Leslie . " 
 " they be happy at their year ! " say the statesman , with a certain 
 pathos . " in the future they see nothing to fear , in the past they have 
 nothing to defend . it be not so with I . " and then , pass on to the 
 vague insinuation or bolder charge against himself and his policy 
 proffer by the precede speaker , Audley gather himself up , and 
 pause ; for his eye here rest on the reporter seat round the 
 table just below he ; and he recognize face not unfamiliar to his 
 recollection when metropolitan assembly have hang on the word which 
 fall from lip then privileged to advise a king . and involuntarily it 
 occur to the ex - minister to escape altogether from this contract 
 audience , - this election , with all its association of pain , - and 
 address himself wholly to that vast and invisible Public , to which those 
 reporter would transmit his idea . at this thought his whole manner 
 gradually change . his eye become fix on the farth verge of the 
 crowd ; his tone grow more solemn in their deep and sonorous swell . he 
 begin to review and to vindicate his whole political life . he speak of 
 the measure he have aid to pass , of his part in the law which now 
 rule the land . he touch lightly , but with pride , on the service 
 he have render to the opinion he have represent . he allude to his 
 neglect of his own private fortune ; but in what detail , however minute , 
 in the public business commit to his charge , could even an enemy 
 accuse he of neglect ? the allusion be no doubt intend to prepare the 
 public for the news that the wealth of Audley Egerton be go . finally , 
 he come to the question that then agitate the day ; and make a general

 foresaw , he should recommend his party to adopt . 

 speak to the motley assembly in that town - hall , Audley 's speech 
 extend to a circle of interest too wide for their sympathy . but that 
 assembly he heed not , - he forget it . the reporter understand he , as 
 their fly pen follow word which they presume neither to correct 
 nor to abridge . Audley 's speech be address to the nation , - the speech 
 of a man in whom the nation yet recognize a chief , desire to clear 
 all misrepresentation from his past career ; calculate , if life be 
 spar to he , on destiny high than he have yet fulfil ; issue a 
 manifesto of principle to be carry later into power , and plant a 
 banner round which the divide section of a broken host might yet rally 
 for battle and for conquest . or perhaps , in the deep of his heart   not 
 even comprehend by reporter , nor to be divine by the public , the 
 uncertainty of life be more feel than the hope of ambition ; and the 
 statesman desire to leave behind he one full vindication of that 
 public integrity and honour , on which , at least , his conscience 
 acknowledge not a stain . 

 " for more than twenty year , " say Audley , in conclusion , " I have know 
 no day in which I have not live for my country . I may at time have 
 oppose the wish of the People , - I may oppose it now ; but , so far as I 
 can form a judgment , only because I prefer their welfare to their wish . 
 and if - as I believe - there have be occasion on which , as one amongst 
 man more renowned , I have amend the law of England , confirm she 
 safety , extend her commerce , uphold her honour , I leave the rest to 
 the censure of my enemy , and   his voice tremble   to the charity of my 
 friend . " 

 before the cheer that greet the close of this speech be over , 
 Richard Avenel arise . what be call " the more respectable part " of 
 an audience - namely , the well educate and well clad , even on the 
 Yellow side of the question - wince a little for the credit of their 
 native borough , when they contemplate the candidate pit against the

 pompous red - tapist , who only think of their salary , and never go

 that we ben't ! " cry the Hundred and fifty , dolorously . " you hear THE 
 PEOPLE ! " say Dick , turn majestically to Egerton , who , with his arm

 you . I repeat here what I once vow on a less public occasion , ' as 
 sure as my name be Richard Avenel , you shall smart for ' - Dick

 Dick sit down . the curve of contempt have pass from Egerton 's lip ; at

 but Randal Leslie next arose , and Audley slowly raise his eye , and

 but if Leonard deal thus respectfully with Egerton , he have no such

 mind accustomed to analyze character and investigate human nature , 
 he detect the varnish insincerity of Randal 's artful address . his

 condition and my labour , - I should feel that he be one of those 
 politician in whom the welfare , the honour , the moral elevation of the 
 people , find no fitting representative . " 

 Leonard sit down amidst great applause , and after a speech that raise 
 the yellow in their own estimation , and materially damage randal

 " thank you , Leslie ; Mr. Fairfield have supply any omission of your , 
 so far as I be concern . and you should excuse he for his attack on 
 yourself , because it may serve to convince you where your fault as a

 " where ? " ask Leslie , with jealous sullenness . 

 " in not believe a single word that you say , " answer Egerton , very 
 dryly ; and then turn away , he say aloud to his proposer , and with 
 a slight sigh , " Mr. Avenel maybe proud of his nephew ! I wish that young 
 man be on our side ; I could train he into a great debater . " 

 and now the proceeding be about to terminate with a show of hand , 
 when a tall , brawny elector in the middle of the hall suddenly arise , 
 and say he have some question to put . a thrill run through the

 Yellow promise by the base art of blue aristocracy , represent in the

 he accept the ungracious invitation . great as have be the curiosity

 have never prove , - strange , indeed , if Blue and Yellow have not strain 
 their ear and hush their breath to listen . 

 it be say that the poet be bear , and the orator make , - a say only 
 partially true . some man have be make poet , and some man have be 
 bear orator . Most probably Harley L'Estrange have hitherto never 
 speak in public ; and he have not now speak five minute before all the 
 passion and humour of the assembly be as much under his command as 
 the key of the instrument be under the hand of the musician . he have 
 take from nature a voice capable of infinite variety of modulation , a 
 countenance of the most flexible play of expression ; and he be keenly 
 alive   as profound humourist be   equally to the ludicrous and the 
 graver side of everything present to his vigorous understanding . 
 Leonard have the eloquence of a poet , Audley Egerton that of a 
 parliamentary debater ; but Harley have the rare gift of eloquence in 
 itself , apart from the matter it convey or adorn , - that gift which 
 demosthene mean by his triple requisite of an orator , which have be 
 improperly translate " action , " but mean in reality " the acting , " " the 
 stage - play . " both Leonard and Audley speak well , from the good sense 
 which their speech contain ; but Harley could have talk nonsense , 
 and make it more effective than sense , - even as a kemble or Macready 
 could produce effect from the trash talk by " the Stranger , " which 
 your merely accomplished performer would fail to extract from the 
 beauty of Hamlet . the art of oratory , indeed , be ally more closely 
 to that of the drama than to any other ; and throughout Harley 's 
 whole nature there run , as the reader may have note   though quite 
 unconsciously to Harley himself , a tendency towards that concentration 
 of thought , action , and circumstance on a single purpose , which make 
 the world form itself into a stage , and gather various and scattered 
 agency into the symmetry and compactness of a drama . this tendency , 
 though it often produce effect that appear artificially theatrical , 
 be not uncommon with person the most genuine and single - minded . it be , 
 indeed , the natural inclination of quick energy spring from warm 
 emotion . hence the very history of nation in their fresh , vigorous , 
 half - civilized youth always shape itself into dramatic form ; while , as 
 the exercise of sober reason expand with civilization , to the injury of 
 the lively faculty and more intuitive impulse , people look to the 
 dramatic form of expression , whether in thought or in action , as if it 
 be the antidote to truth , instead of be its abstract and essence . 

 but to return from this long and somewhat metaphysical digression : 
 whatever might be the cause why Harley L'Estrange speak so wonderfully 
 well , there could be no doubt that wonderfully well he do speak . he 
 turn the demagogue and his attack into the most felicitous ridicule , 
 and yet with the most genial good - humour ; describe that virtuous 
 gentleman 's adventure in search of corruption through the pure region 
 of Fish Lane and the Back Slums ; and then sum up the evidence on 
 which the demagogue have found his charge , with a humour so caustic and 
 original that the audience be convulse with laughter . from laughter 
 Harley hurry his audience almost to the pathos of tear , - for he 
 speak of the insinuation against his father so that every son and every 
 father in the assembly feel move as at the voice of Nature . 

 a turn in a sentence , and a new emotion seize the assembly . Harley be 
 identify himself with the Lansmere elector . he speak of his pride in 
 be a Lansmere man , and all the Lansmere elector suddenly feel proud 
 of he . he talk with familiar kindness of old friend remember in 
 his schoolboy holiday , rejoice to find so many alive and prospering . 
 he have a felicitous word to each . 

 " dear old Lansmere ! " say he , and the simple exclamation win he the 
 heart of all . in fine , when he pause , as if to retire , it be amidst a 
 storm of acclamation . Audley grasp his hand , and whisper , " I be 
 the only one here not surprised , Harley . now you have discover your 
 power , never again let they slumber . what a life may be yours if you no 
 long waste it ! " Harley extricate his hand , and his eye glitter . he 
 make a sign that he have more to say , and the applause be hush . " my 
 right honourable friend chide I for the year that I have waste . 
 true ; my year have be waste , - no matter how nor wherefore ! but his ! 
 how have they be spend ? in such devotion to the public that those 
 who know he not as I do , have say that he have not one feeling leave to 
 spare to the obscurer duty and more limited affection , by which man 
 of ordinary talent and humble mind rivet the link of that social 
 order which it be the august destiny of statesmen - like he who now sit 
 beside I - to cherish and defend . but , for my part , I think that there 
 be no be so dangerous as the solemn hypocrite , who , because he 
 drill his cold nature into serve mechanically some conventional 
 abstraction , - whether he call it ' the Constitution ' or ' the 
 Public , ' - hold himself dispense from whatever , in the warm blood of 
 private life , win attachment to goodness , and confidence to truth . let 
 other , then , praise my right honourable friend as the incorruptible 
 politician . pardon I if I draw his likeness as the loyal sincere man , 
 who might say with the honest priest ' that he could not tell a lie to 
 gain heaven by it ! ' - and with so fine a sense of honour , that he would 
 hold it a lie merely to conceal the truth . " Harley then draw a brilliant 
 picture of the type of chivalrous honesty , - of the ideal which the 
 English attach to the phrase of " a perfect gentleman , " apply each 
 sentence to his Right honourable friend with an emphasis that seem 
 to burst from his heart . to all of the audience , save two , it be an 
 eulogium which the fervent sincerity of the eulogist alone save from 
 hyperbole . but Levy rub his hand , and chuckle inly ; and Egerton 
 hang his head , and move restlessly on his seat . every word that Harley 
 utter lodge an arrow in Audley 's breast . amidst the cheer that 
 follow this admirable sketch of the " loyal man , " Harley recognize 
 Leonard 's enthusiastic voice . he turn sharply towards the young 
 man : " Mr. Fairfield cheer this description of integrity , and its 
 application ; let he imitate the model set before he , and he may live 
 to hear praise as genuine as mine from some friend who have test his 
 worth as I have test Mr. Egerton 's . Mr. Fairfield be a poet : his 
 claim to that title be dispute by one of the speaker who precede 
 I ! - unjustly dispute ! Mr. Fairfield be every inch a poet . but , it have 
 be ask , ' be poet fit for the business of senate ? will they not 
 be write sonnet to Peggy and Moggy , when you want they to concentrate 
 their divine imagination on the detail of a beer bill ? ' do not let 
 Mr. Fairfield 's friend be alarm . at the risk of injury to the two 
 candidate whose cause I espouse , truth compel I to say , that poet , 
 when they stoop to action , be not less prosaic than the dull amongst 
 we ; they be sway by the same selfish interest , they be move by 
 the same petty passion . it be a mistake to suppose that any detail in 
 common life , whether in public or private , can be too mean to seduce 
 the exquisite pliance of their fancy . Nay , in public life , we may trust 
 they well than other man ; for vanity be a kind of second conscience , 
 and , as a poet have himself say , - 

  " ' who fear not to do ill , yet fear the name , 
  and free from conscience , be a slave to shame . ' 

 in private life alone we do well to be on our guard against these 
 child of fancy , for they so devote to the Muse all their treasury 
 of sentiment , that we can no more expect they to waste a thought on the 
 plain duty of man , than we can expect the spendthrift , who dazzle the 
 town , ' to fritter away his money in pay his debt . ' but all the world 
 be agree to be indulgent to the infirmity of those who be their 
 own deceiver and their own chastiser . poet have more enthusiasm , more 
 affection , more heart than other ; but only for fiction of their own 
 creating . it be in vain for we to attach they to ourselves by vulgar 
 merit , by commonplace obligation , strive and sacrifice as we may . they 
 be ungrateful to we , only because gratitude be so very unpoetical a 
 subject . we lose they the moment we attempt to bind . their love - 

  " ' light as air , at sight of human tie , 
  spread its light wing , and in a moment fly . ' 

 " they follow their own caprice , adore their own delusion , and , deeming 
 the form of humanity too material for their fantastic affection , 
 conjure up a ghost , and be chill to death by its embrace ! " 

 then , suddenly aware that he be pass beyond the comprehension of his 
 audience , and touch upon the bound of his bitter secret   for here he 
 be think , not of Leonard , but of Nora , Harley give a new and more 
 homely direction to his terrible irony , - turn into tell ridicule 
 the most elevated sentiment Leonard 's speech have convey , hasten on 
 to a rapid view of political question in general , defend Leslie 
 with the same apparent earnestness and latent satire with which he have 
 eulogize Audley , and conclude a speech which , for popular effect , 
 have never be equal in that hall , amidst a diapason of cheer that 
 threaten to bring down the rafter . 

 in a few minute more the proceeding be close , a show of hand 
 take . the show be declare by the Mayor , who be a thorough Blue , in 
 favour of the Right Hon . Audley Egerton and Randal Leslie , Esquire . 

 cry of " no , " " Shame , " " Partial , " etc . , a poll demand on behalf of 
 the other two candidate , and the crowd begin to pour out of the hall . 

 Harley be the first who vanish , retreat by the private entrance . 
 Egerton follow ; randal lingering , Avenel come up and shake hand with 
 he openly , but whisper privately , " meet I to - night in Lansmere Park , 
 in the oak copse , about three hundred yard from the turnstile , at 
 the town end of the park . we must see how to make all right . what a 
 confound humbug this have be ! " 




 CHAPTER XXVI . 

 if the vigour of Harley 's address have take by surprise both friend

 CHAPTER XXVII . 

 the lead member of the Blue Committee be invite to dine at the

 the party be gay and animate , despite the absence of Audley Egerton ,

 " and you think his nephew will be withdraw ? poor young man ! defeat at 
 his age , and with such talent , be hard to bear . " Audley sigh . 

 " I must leave you now , if you have nothing important to say , " say the 
 baron , rise . " I have much to do , as the election be yet to be win , 
 and - to you the loss of it would be - " 

 " Ruin , I know . well , Levy , it be , on the whole , to your advantage that 
 I should not lose . there may be more to get from I yet . and , judge by 
 the letter I receive this morning , my position be render so safe by 
 the absolute necessity of my party to keep I up , that the news of my 
 pecuniary difficulty will not affect I so much as I once fear . 
 never be my career so free from obstacle , so clear towards the high 
 summit of ambition ; never , in my day of ostentatious magnificence , as 
 it be now , when I be prepared to shrink into a lodging , with a single 
 servant . " 

 " I be glad to hear it ; and I be the more anxious to secure your 
 election , upon which this career must depend , because - nay , I hardly 
 like to tell you - " 

 " speak on . " 

 " I have be oblige , by a sudden rush on all my resource , to consign 
 some of your bill and promissory note to another , who , if your person 
 should not be protect from arrest by parliamentary privilege , might be 
 harsh and - " 

 " traitor ! " interrupt Egerton , fiercely , all the compose contempt with 
 which he usually treat the usurer give way , ' say no more . how could 
 I ever expect otherwise ! you have foresee my defeat , and have plan 
 my destruction . presume no reply ! Sir , begone from my presence ! " 

 " you will find that you have bad friend than myself , " say the baron , 
 move to the door ; " and if you be defeat , if your prospect for 
 life be destroy , I be the last man you will think of blame . but 
 I forgive your anger , and trust that to - morrow you will receive those 
 explanation of my conduct which you be now in no temper to bear . I go 
 to take care of the election . " 

 leave alone , Audley 's sudden passion seem to forsake he . 

 he gather together , in that prompt and logical precision which the 
 habit of transact public business bestow , all his thought , and 
 sound all his fear ; and most vivid of every thought , and most 
 intolerable of every fear , be the belief that the baron have betray 
 he to L'Estrange . 

 " I can not bear this suspense , " he cry aloud and abruptly . " I will see 
 Harley myself . open as he be , the very sound of his voice will tell I 
 at once if I be a bankrupt even of human friendship . if that friendship 
 be secure , if Harley yet clasp my hand with the same cordial warmth , all 
 other loss shall not wring from my fortitude one complaint . " 

 he ring the bell ; his valet , who be wait in the anteroom , appear . 

 " go and see if Lord L'Estrange be engage . I would speak with he . " 

 the servant come back in less than two minute . 

 " I find that my Lord be now particularly engage , since he have give 
 strict order that he be not to be disturb . " 

 " engage ! on what , whom with ? " 

 " he be in his own room , sir , with a clergyman , who arrive , and din 
 here , to - day . I be tell that he be formerly curate of Lansmere . " 

 " Lansmere ! curate ! his name , his name ! not Dale ? " 

 " yes , sir , that be the name , - the Reverend Mr. Dale . " 

 " leave I , " say Audley , in a faint voice . " Dale ! the man who suspect 
 Harley , who call on I in London , speak of a child , - my child , - and 
 send I to find but another grave ! he closet with Harley , - he ! " 

 Audley sink back on his chair , and literally gasp for breath . few 
 man in the world have a more establish reputation for the courage that 
 dignifie manhood , whether the physical courage or the moral . but at 
 that moment it be not grief , not remorse , that paralyzed Audley , - it 
 be fear . the brave man see before he , as a thing visible and menacing , 
 the aspect of his own treachery , - that crime of a coward ; and into 
 cowardice he be stricken . what have he to dread ? nothing save the 
 accuse face of an injure friend , - nothing but that . and what more 
 terrible ? the only being , amidst all his pomp of partisan , who survive 
 to love he , the only be for whom the cold statesman feel the happy , 
 living , human tenderness of private affection , lose to he forever ! 
 he cover his face with both hand , and sit in suspense of something 
 awful , as a child sit in the dark , the drop on his brow , and his frame 
 trembling . 




 CHAPTER XXVIII . 

 meanwhile Harley have listen to Mr. Dale 's vindication of Leonard with 
 cold attention . 

 " Enough , " say he , at the close . " Mr. Fairfield   for so we will yet call 
 he   shall see I to - night ; and if apology be due to he , I will make 
 it . at the same time , it shall be decide whether he continue this 
 contest or retire . and now , Mr. Dale , it be not to hear how this young 
 man woo , or shrink from wooing , my affianced bride , that I avail 
 myself of your promise to visit I at this house . we agree that the 
 seducer of Nora Avenel deserved chastisement , and I promise that 
 Nora Avenel 's son should find a father . both these assurance shall be 
 fulfil to - morrow . and you , sir , " continue Harley , rise , his whole 
 form gradually enlarge by the dignity of passion , " who wear the garb 
 appropriate to the holy office of christian charity ; you who have 
 presume to think that , before the beard have darken my cheek , I could 
 first betray the girl who have be rear under this roof , then abandon 
 she , - sneak like a dastard from the place in which my victim come to 
 die , leave my own son , by the woman thus wrong , without thought or 
 care , through the perilous year of tempt youth , till I find he , by 
 chance , an outcast in a desert more dread than Hagar 's , - you , sir , who 
 have for long year thus judge of I , shall have the occasion to direct 
 your holy anger towards the rightful head ; and in I , you who have 
 condemn the culprit shall respect the judge . " 

 Mr. Dale be at first startle , and almost awed , by this unexpected 
 burst . but , accustom to deal with the stern and the darkest 
 passion , his calm sense and his habit of authority over those whose 
 soul be bar to he , nobly recover from their surprise . " my Lord , " 
 say he , " first , with humility I bow to your rebuke , and entreat your 
 pardon for my erring , and , as you say , my uncharitable opinion . 
 we dweller in a village and obscure pastor of a humble flock , we , 
 mercifully remove from temptation , be too apt , perhaps , to exaggerate 
 its power over those whose lot be cast in that great world which have 
 so many gate ever open to evil . this be my sole excuse if I be mislead 
 by what appear to I strong circumstantial evidence . but forgive I 
 again if I warn you not to fall into an error perhaps little light 
 than my own . your passion , when you clear yourself from reproach , 
 become you . but ah , my Lord , when with that stern brow and those 
 flash eye , you launch your menace upon another over whom you would 
 constitute yourself the judge , forgetful of the divine precept , 
 ' Judge not , ' I feel that I be listen no long to honest 
 self - vindication , - I feel that I be listen to fierce revenge ! " 

 " call it revenge , or what you will , " say Harley , with sullen firmness ; 
 " but I have be sting too deeply not to ste . Frank with all , till the 
 last few day , I have ever be . Frank to you , at least , even now , 
 this much I tell you : I pretend to no virtue in what I still hold to be 
 justice ; but no declamation nor homily tend to prove that justice 
 be sinful will move my resolve . as man I have be outraged , and as 
 man I will retaliate . the way and the mode , the true criminal and his 
 fitting sentence , you will soon learn , sir . I have much to do to - night ; 
 forgive I if I adjourn for the present all further conference . " 

 " no , no ; do not dismiss I . there be something , in spite of your present 
 language , which so command my interest ; I see that there have be so 
 much suffering where there be now so much wrath , - that I would save you 
 from the suffering bad than all , - remorse . oh , pause , my dear Lord , 
 pause and answer I but two question ; then I will leave your after 
 course to yourself . " 

 " say on , sir , " say Lord L'Estrange , touch , and with respect . 

 " first ; then , analyze your own feeling . be this anger merely to punish 
 an offender and to right the living , - for who can pretend to right the 
 dead ? or be there not some private hate that stir and animate and 
 confuse all ? " 

 Harley remain silent . Mr. Dale renew , 

 " you love this poor girl . your language even now reveal it . you speak 
 of treachery : perhaps you have a rival who deceive you ; I know not , 
 guess not , whom . but if you would strike the rival , must you not wound 
 the innocent son ? and , in present Nora 's child to his father , as 
 you pledge yourself to do , can you mean some cruel mockery that , under 
 seem kindness , imply some unnatural vengeance ? " 

 " you read well the heart of man , " say Harley ; " and I have own to you 
 that I be but man . pass on ; you have another question . " 

 " and one more solemn and important . in my world of a village , revenge be 
 a common passion ; it be the sin of the uninstructed . the savage deem 
 it noble ! but Christ 's religion , which be the sublime Civilizer , 
 emphatically condemn it . why ? because religion ever seek to ennoble 
 a man ; and nothing so debase he as revenge . look into your own heart , 
 and tell I whether , since you have cherish this passion , you have not 
 feel all sense of right and wrong confuse , - have not feel that whatever 
 would before have seem to you mean and base , appear now but just 
 mean to your heated end . revenge be ever a hypocrite : rage , at least , 
 strike with the naked sword ; but revenge , stealthy and patient , 
 conceal the weapon of the assassin . my Lord , your colour change . what 
 be your answer to my question ? " 

 " oh , " exclaim Harley , with a voice thrilling in its mournful anguish , 
 " it be not since I have cherish the revenge that I be change , that 
 right and wrong grow dark to I , that hypocrisy seem the atmosphere fit 
 for earth . no ; it be since the discovery that demand the vengeance . it 
 be useless , sir , " he continue impetuously , - " useless to argue with I . 
 be I to sit down , patient and impotent , under the sense of the wrong 
 which I have receive , I should feel , indeed , that debasement which you 
 ascribe to the gratification of what you term revenge . I should never 
 regain the self - esteem which the sentiment of power now restore to I ; 
 I should feel as if the whole world could perceive and jeer at my meek 
 humiliation . I know not why I have say so much , - why I have betray 
 to you so much of my secret mind , and stoop to vindicate my purpose . I 
 never mean it . again I say , we must close this conference . " Harley here 
 walk to the door , and open it significantly . 

 " one word more , Lord L'Estrange , - but one . you will not hear I . I be a 
 comparative stranger , but you have a friend , a friend dear and intimate , 
 now under the same roof . will you consent , at least , to take counsel 
 of Mr. Audley Egerton ? none can doubt his friendship for you ; none 
 can doubt that whatever he advise will be that which well become your 
 honour . what , my Lord , you hesitate , - you feel ashamed to confide to 
 your dear friend a purpose which his mind would condemn ? then I 
 will seek he , I will implore he to save you from what can but entail 
 repentance . " 

 " Mr. Dale , I must forbid you to see Mr. Egerton . what have pass between 
 we ought to be as sacred to you as a priest of Rome hold confession . 
 this much , however , I will say to content you : I promise that I will 
 do nothing that shall render I unworthy of Mr. Audley Egerton 's 
 friendship , or which his fine sense of honour shall justify he in 
 blaming . let that satisfy you . " 

 " ah , my Lord , " cry Mr. Dale , pausing irresolute at the doorway , and 
 seize Harley 's hand , " I should indeed be satisfied if you would submit 
 yourself to high counsel than mine , - than Mr. Egerton 's , than man 's . 
 have you never feel the efficacy of prayer ? " 

 " my life have be waste , " reply Harley , " and I dare not , therefore , 
 boast that I have find prayer efficacious . but , so far back as I can 
 remember , it have at least be my habit to pray to Heaven , night and 
 morning , until at least - until - " the natural and obstinate candour of 
 the man force out the last word , which imply reservation . he stop 
 short . 

 " until you have cherish revenge ? you have not dare to pray since ? 
 oh , reflect what evil there be within we , when we dare not come before 
 Heaven , - dare not pray for what we wish . you be move . I leave you to 
 your own thought . " Harley incline his head , and the parson pass he 
 by , and leave he alone , - startle indeed ; but be he soften ? as Mr. 
 Dale hurry along the corridor , much agitated , Violante steal from a 
 recess form by a large bay window , and link her arm in his , say 
 anxiously , but timidly : " I have be wait for you , dear Mr. Dale ; and 
 so long ! you have be with Lord L'Estrange ? " 

 " well ! " 

 " why do you not speak ? you have leave he comfort , happy ? " 

 " Happier ! no . " 

 " what ! " say Violante , with a look of surprise , and a sadness not 
 unmixe with petulance in her quick tone . " what ! do he then so grieve 
 that Helen prefer another ? " 

 despite the grave emotion that disturb his mind , Mr. Dale be strike 
 by Violante 's question , and the voice in which it be say . he love 
 she tenderly . " child , child , " say he , " I be glad that Helen have escape 
 Lord L'Estrange . Beware , oh , beware how he excite any gentler interest 
 in yourself . he be a dangerous man , - more dangerous for glimpse of a 
 fine original nature . he may well move the heart of the innocent and 
 inexperienced , for he have strangely creep into mine . but his heart be 
 swollen with pride and ire and malice . " 

 " you mistake ; it be false ! " cry Violante , impetuously . " I can not 
 believe one word that would asperse he who have save my father from a 
 prison , or from death . you have not treat he gently . he fancy he 
 have be wrong by Leonard , receive ingratitude from Helen . he have 
 feel the sting in proportion to his own susceptible and generous heart , 
 and you have chide where you should have soothe . poor Lord L'Estrange ! 
 and you have leave he still indignant and unhappy ? " 

 " foolish girl ! I have leave he meditate sin ; I have leave he afraid to 
 pray ; I have leave he on the brink of some design - I know not what - but 
 which involve more than Leonard in project of revenge ; I have leave he 
 so , that if his heart be really susceptible and generous , he will wake 
 from wrath to be the victim of long and unavaile remorse . if your 
 father have influence over he , tell Dr. Riccabocca what I say , and bid 
 he seek , and in his turn save , the man who save himself . he have not 
 listen to religion , - he maybe more docile to philosophy . I can not stay 
 here long , - I must go to Leonard . " 

 Mr. Dale break from Violante and hurry down the corridor ; Violante 
 stand on the same spot , stunned and breathless . Harley on the brink of 
 some strange sin ! Harley to wake , the victim of remorse ! Harley to be 
 save , as he have save her father ! her breast heave , her colour go 
 and come , her eye be raise , her lip murmur . she advance with 
 soft footstep up the corridor ; she see the light gleam from 
 Harley 's room , and suddenly they be darken , as the inmate of the 
 room shut to the door , with angry and impatient hand . 

 an outward act often betray the inward mind . as Harley have thus close 
 the door , so have he seek to shut his heart from the intrusion of 
 soft and holier thought . he have turn to his hearthstone , and 
 stand on it , resolve and harden . the man who have love with such 
 pertinacious fidelity far so many year could not at once part with 
 hate . a passion once admit to his breast , clung to it with such 
 rooted force ! but woe , woe to thee , Harley L'Estrange , if tomorrow 
 at this hour thou stand at the hearthstone , thy design accomplish , 
 know that , in the fulfilment of thy blind will , thou hast meet 
 falsehood with falsehood , and deception with deceit ! what though those 
 design now seem so consummate , so just , so appropriate , so exquisite a 
 revenge , - seem to thee the sole revenge wit can plan and civilized life 
 allow : wilt thou ever wash from thy memory the stain that will sully 
 thine honour ? Thou , too , profess friendship still , and mask 
 perfidy under smile ! grant that the wrong be great as thou deem it , - be 
 ten time great : the sense of thy meanness , o gentleman and soldier , 
 will bring the blush to thy cheek in the depth of thy solitude . Thou , 
 who now thinkest other unworthy a trustful love , wilt feel thyself 
 forever unworthy theirs . Thy seclusion will know not repose . the dignity 
 of man will forsake thee . Thy proud eye will quail from the gaze . Thy 
 step will no long spurn the earth that it tread on . he who have 
 once do a base thing be never again wholly reconcile to honour . and 
 woe - thrice woe , if thou learn too late that thou hast exaggerated thy 
 fancy wrong : that there be excuse , where thou se none ; that thy 
 friend may have err , but that his error be venial compare to thy 
 fancied retribution ! 

 thus , however , in the superb elation of conscious power , though lavish 
 on a miserable object , - a terrible example of what change one evil and 
 hateful thought , cherish to the exclusion of all other , can make 
 in the noble nature , stand , on the hearth of his father , and on the 
 abyss of a sorrow and a shame from which there could be no recall , the 
 determined and scornful man . 

 a hand be on the door , - he do not hear it ; a form pass the 
 threshold,-he do not see it ; a light step pause , a soft eye gaze . 
 Deaf and blind still to both . 

 Violante come on , gather courage , and stand at the hearth by his 
 side . 




 CHAPTER XXIX . 

 " LORD L'ESTRANGE , noble friend ! " 

 " you ! - and here - Violante ? be it I whom you seek ? for what ? good 
 heaven ! what have happen ? why be you so pale ; why tremble ? " 

 " have you forgive Helen ? " ask Violante , begin with evasive 
 question , and her cheek be pale no more . " Helen , the poor child ! I have 
 nothing in she to forgive , much to thank she for . she have be frank and 
 honest . " 

 " and Leonard - whom I remember in my childhood - you have forgive he ? " 

 " fair mediator , " say Harley , smile , though coldly , " happy be the man 
 who deceive another ; all plead for he . and if the man deceive can not 
 forgive , no one will sympathize or excuse . " 

 " but Leonard do not deceive you ? " 

 " yes , from the first . it be a long tale , and not to be tell to you ; but 
 I can not forgive he . " 

 " Adieu ! my Lord . Helen must , then , still be very dear to you ! " Violante 
 turn away . her emotion be so artless , her very anger so charming , 
 that the love , against which , in the prevalence of his later and dark 
 passion , he have so sternly struggle , rush back upon Harley 's breast ; 
 but it come only in storm . 

 " stay , but talk not of Helen ! " he exclaim . " ah , if Leonard 's sole 
 offence have be what you appear to deem it , do you think I could feel 
 resentment ? no ; I should have gratefully hail the hand that sever a 
 rash and ungenial tie . I would have give my ward to her lover with such 
 a dower as it suit my wealth to bestow . but his offence date from 
 his very birth . to bless and to enrich the son of a man who - Violante , 
 listen to I . we may soon part , and forever . other may misconstrue my 
 action ; you , at least , shall know from what just principle they spring . 
 there be a man whom I single out of the world as more than a brother . 
 in the romance of my boyhood I see one who dazzle my fancy , captivate 
 my heart . it be a dream of Beauty breathe into wake life . I 
 love , - I believe myself belove . I confide all my heart to this 
 friend , - this more than brother ; he undertake to befriend and to aid 
 my suit . on that very pretext he first see this ill - fat girl , see , 
 betray , destroy she ; leave I ignorant that her love , which I have 
 think mine , have be lavish so wildly on another ; leave I to believe 
 that my own suit she have flee , but in generous self - sacrifice , - for 
 she be poor and humbly bear ; that - oh , vain idiot that I be ! - the 
 self - sacrifice have be too strong for a young human heart , which have 
 break in the struggle ; leave I to corrode my spring of life in remorse ; 
 clasp my hand in mocking comfort , smile at my tear of agony - not one 
 tear himself for his own poor victim ! and suddenly , not long since , I 
 learn all this . and in the father of Leonard Fairfield , you behold 
 the man who have poison all the well - spring of joy to I . you weep ! 
 oh , Violante ! the Past he have blight and embitter , - that I could 
 forgive ; but the Future be blast too . for just ere this treason 
 be reveal to I , I have begin to awake from the torpor of my dreary 
 penance , to look with fortitude towards the duty I have slight , to 
 own that the pilgrimage before I be not barren . and then , oh then , I 
 feel that all love be not bury in a grave . I feel that you , have 
 fate so grant , might have be all to my manhood which youth only 
 see through the delusion of its golden mist . true , I be then bind 
 to Helen ; true , that honour to she might forbid I all hope . but still , 
 even to know that my heart be not all ash , that I could love again , 
 that that glorious power and privilege of our being be still mine , 
 seem to I so heavenly sweet . but then this revelation of falsehood 
 burst on I , and all truth seem blot from the universe . I be free 
 from Helen ; ah , freed , forsooth , - because not even rank and wealth , and 
 benefit and confide tenderness , could bind to I one human heart ! 
 free from she ; but between I and your fresh nature stand Suspicion as 
 an Upas tree . not a hope that would pass through the tainted air and fly 
 to you , but fall dead under the dismal bough . I love ! 

 " ha , ha ! I - I , whom the past have teach the impossibility to be love 
 again . no : if those soft lip murmur ' yes ' to the burn prayer that , 
 have I be free but two short week ago , would have rush from 
 the frank deep of my heart , I should but imagine that you deceive 
 yourself , - a girl 's first fleeting delusive fancy , - nothing more ! be 
 you my bride , Violante , I should but debase your bright nature by my own 
 curse of distrust . at each word of tenderness , my heart would say , ' how 
 long will this last ; when will the deception come ? ' your beauty , your 
 gift , would bring I but jealous terror , eternally I should fly from 
 the Present to the Future , and say . ' these hair will be gray , while 
 flattering youth will surround she in the zenith of her charm . ' why 
 then do I hate and curse my foe ? why do I resolve upon revenge ? I 
 comprehend it now . I know that there be something more imperious than 
 the ghost of the past that urge I on . gaze on you , I feel that it 
 be the dim sense of a mighty and priceless loss ; it be not the dead 
 Nora , - it be the live Violante . look not at I with those reproachful 
 eye : they can not reverse my purpose ; they can not banish suspicion from 
 my sickened soul ; they can not create a sunshine in the midst of this 
 ghastly twilight . go , go ; leave I to the sole joy that bequeath no 
 disappointment , the sole feeling that unite I to social man ; leave I 
 to my revenge . " 

 " revenge ! oh , cruel ! " exclaim Violante , lay her hand on his arm . 
 " and in revenge , it be your own life that you will risk ! " 

 " my life , simple child ! this be no contest of life against life . could I 
 bare to all the world my wrong for their ribald laughter , I should only 
 give to my foe the triumph to pity my frenzy , to shun the contest ; or 
 grant it , if I could find a second - and then fire in the air . and all 
 the world would say , ' Generous Egerton ! soul of honour ! ' " 

 " Egerton , Mr. Egerton ! he can not be this foe ? it be not on he you can 
 design revenge , - you who spend all your hour in serve his cause , you 
 to whom he trust so fondly , you who lean yesterday on his shoulder , 
 and smile so cheeringly in his face ? " 

 " do I ? Hypocrisy against hypocrisy , snare against snare : that be my 
 revenge . " 

 " Harley , Harley ! Cease , cease ! " 

 the storm of passion rush on unheede . 

 " I seem to promote his ambition but to crush it into the mire . I have 
 deliver he from the gentle gripe of an usurer , so that he shall hold 
 at my option alm or a prison - " 

 " Friend , friend ! hush , hush ! " 

 " I have make the youth he have rear and foster into treachery like 
 his own   your father 's precious choice , Randal Leslie   mine instrument 
 in the gall lesson how ingratitude can sting . his very son shall 
 avenge the mother , and be lead to his father 's breast as victor , with 
 Randal Leslie , in the contest that deprive sire and benefactor of all 
 that make life dear to ambitious egotism . and if , in the breast of 
 Audley Egerton , there can yet lurk one memory of what I be to he 
 and to truth , not his least punishment will be the sense that his own 
 perfidy have so change the man whose very scorn of falsehood have teach 
 he to find in fraud itself the power of retribution . " 

 " if this be not a terrible dream , " murmur Violante , recoil , " it be 
 not your foe alone that you will deprive of all that make life dear . 
 Act thus - and what , in the future , be leave to I ? " 

 " to you ? oh , never fear . I may give Randal Leslie a triumph over his 
 patron , but in the same hour I will unmask his villany , and sweep 
 he forever from your path . what in the future be leave to you ? - your 
 birthright and your native land ; hope , joy , love , felicity . could it be 
 possible that in the soft but sunny fancy which play round the heart 
 of maiden youth , but still send no warmth into its deep , - could it be 
 possible that you have honour I with a gentler thought , it will pass 
 away , and you will be the pride and delight of one of your own year , to 
 whom the vista of Time be haunt by no chill spectre , one who can 
 look upon that lovely face , and not turn away to mutter , ' too fair , too 
 fair for I ! ' " 

 " oh , agony ! " exclaim Violante , with sudden passion . " in my turn hear 
 I . if , as you promise , I be release from the dreadful thought that he , 
 at whose touch I shudder , can claim this hand , my choice be irrevocably 
 make . the altar which await I will not be those of a human love . but 
 oh , I implore you - by all the memory of your own life , hitherto , if 
 sorrowful , unsullied , by the generous interest you yet profess for 
 I , whom you will have twice save from a danger to which death be 
 mercy - leave , oh , leave to I the right to regard your image as I have 
 do from the first dawn of childhood . leave I the right to honour and 
 revere it . let not an act accompany with a meanness - oh that I should 
 say the word ! - a meanness and a cruelty that give the lie to your whole 
 life - make even a grateful remembrance of you an unworthy sin . when I 
 kneel within the wall that divide I from the world , oh , let I think 
 that I can pray for you as the noble be that the world contain ! 
 hear I ! hear I ! " 

 " Violante ! " murmur Harley , his whole frame heave with emotion , " bear 
 with I . do not ask of I the sacrifice of what seem to I the cause 
 of manhood itself , - to sit down , meek and patient , under a wrong that 
 debase I , with the consciousness that all my life I have be the 
 miserable dupe to affection I deem so honest , to regret that I 
 believe so holy . ah , I should feel more mean in my pardon than you can 
 think I in revenge ! be it an acknowledge enemy , I could open my arm 
 to he at your bidding ; but the perfidious friend ! - ask it not . my 
 cheek burn at the thought , as at the stain of a blow . give I but 
 to - morrow - one day - I demand no more - wholly to myself and to the past , 
 and mould I for the future as you will . Pardon , pardon the ungenerous 
 thought that extend distrust to you . I retract they ; they be 
 go , - dispel before those touching word , those ingenuous eye . at 
 your foot , Violante , I repent and I implore ! your father himself shall 
 banish your sordid suitor . before this hour to - morrow you will be free . 
 oh , then , then ! will you not give I this hand to guide I again into 
 the paradise of my youth ? Violante , it be in vain to wrestle with 
 myself , to doubt , to reason , to be wisely fearful ! I love , I love you ! I 
 trust again in virtue and faith . I place my fate in your keeping . " if 
 at time Violante may appear to have venture beyond the limit of strict 
 maiden bashfulness , much may be ascribe to her habitual candour , her 
 solitary rearing , and remoteness from the world , the very innocence of 
 her soul , and the warmth of heart which Italy give its daughter . but 
 now that sublimity of thought and purpose which pervade her nature , 
 and require only circumstance to develop , make she superior to all the 
 prompting of love itself . dream realize which she have scarcely dare 
 to own ; Harley free , Harley at her foot ; all the woman struggle at her 
 heart , mantle in her blush , still strong than love , strong than 
 the joy of be love again , be the heroic will , - will to save he , 
 who in all else rule her existence , from the eternal degradation to 
 which passion have blind his own confused and war spirit . 

 leave one hand in his impassioned clasp , as he still knelt before she , 
 she raise on high the other . " ah , " she say , scarce audibly , - " ah , if 
 heaven vouchsafe I the proud and blissful privilege to be ally to 
 your fate , to minister to your happiness , never should I know one fear 
 of your distrust . no time , no change , no sorrow - not even the loss of 
 your affection - could make I forfeit the right to remember that you have 
 once confide to I a heart so noble . but " - here her voice rise in its 
 tone , and the glow flee from her cheek - " but , o Thou the ever present , 
 hear and receive the solemn vow . if to I he refuse to sacrifice the sin 
 that would debase he , that sin be the barrier between we evermore ; 
 and may my life , devote to Thy service , atone for the hour in which 
 he belie the nature he receive from Thee ! Harley , release I ! I have 
 speak : firm as yourself , I leave the choice to you . " 

 " you judge I harshly , " say Harley , rise , with sullen anger ; " but at 
 least I have not the meanness to sell what I hold as justice , though the 
 bribe may include my last hope of happiness . " 

 " Meanness ! oh , unhappy , belove Harley ! " exclaim Violante , with such 
 a gush of exquisite reproachful tenderness , that it thrill he as 
 the voice of the parting guardian angel . " Meanness ! but it be that from 
 which I implore you to save yourself . you can not judge , you can not see . 
 you be dark , dark . lose Christian that you be , what bad than 
 heathen darkness to feign the friendship the well to betray ; to punish 
 falsehood by become yourself so false ; to accept the confidence even 
 of your bitter foe , and then to sink below his own level in deceit ? 
 and oh , bad than all - to threaten that a son - son of the woman you 
 profess to love - should swell your vengeance against a father ! no ! it 
 be not you that say this , - it be the fiend ! " 

 " enough ! " exclaim Harley , startle , conscience - stricken , and rush 
 into resentment , in order to escape the sense of shame . " enough ! you 
 insult the man you profess to honour . " 

 " I honour the prototype of gentleness and valour . I honour one who 
 seem to I to clothe with life every grand and generous image that be 
 bear from the soul of poet . destroy that ideal , and you destroy the 
 Harley whom I honour . he be dead to I forever . I will mourn for he 
 as his widow , faithful to his memory , weep over the thought of what 
 he be . " Sobs choke her voice ; but as Harley , once more melt , sprang 
 forward to regain her side , she escape with a yet quick movement , 
 gain the door , and dart down the corridor , vanish from his sight . 

 Harley stand still one moment , thoroughly irresolute , nay , almost 
 subdue . then sternness , though less rigid than before , gradually come 
 to his brow . the demon have still its hold in the stubborn and marvellous 
 pertinacity with which the man clung to all that once strike root at his 
 heart . with a sudden impulse that still withhold decision , yet speak 
 of sore - shake purpose , he stride to his desk , draw from it Nora 's 
 manuscript , and pass from his room . 

 Harley have mean never to have reveal to Audley the secret he 
 have gain until the moment when revenge be consummate . he have 
 contemplate no vain reproach . his wrath would have speak forth in 
 deed , and then a word would have suffice as the key to all . willing , 
 perhaps , to hail some extenuation of perfidy , though the possibility 
 of such extenuation he have never before admit , he determine on 
 the interview which he have hitherto so obstinately shun , and go 
 straight to the room in which Audley Egerton still sit , solitary and 
 fearful . 




 CHAPTER XXX . 

 Egerton hear the well - know step advance near and nearer up the 
 corridor , hear the door open and reclose ; and he feel , by one of those 
 strange and unaccountable instinct which we call foreboding , that the 
 hour he have dread for so many secret year have come at last . he nerve 
 his courage , withdraw his hand from his face , and rise in silence . 

 no less silent , Harley stand before he . the two man gaze on each 
 other ; you might have hear their breathing . 

 " you have see Mr. Dale ? " say Egerton , at length . " you know - " 

 " All ! " say Harley , complete the arrest sentence . Audley draw a long 
 sigh . " be it so ; but no , Harley , you deceive yourself ; you can not know 
 all , from any one living , save myself . " 

 " my knowledge come from the dead , " answer Harley , and the fatal 
 memoir drop from his hand upon the table . the leaf fall with a 
 dull , low sound , mournful and faint as might be the tread of a ghost , if 
 the tread give sound . they fall , those still confession of an obscure , 
 uncomprehended life , amidst letter and document eloquent of the strife 
 that be then agitate million , - the fleeting , turbulent fear and 
 hop that torture party and perplex a nation ; the stormy business 
 of practical public life , so remote from individual love and individual 
 sorrow . 

 Egerton 's eye see they fall . the room be but partially light . at 
 the distance where he stand , he do not recognize the character ; but 
 involuntarily he shiver , and involuntarily draw near . 

 " hold yet awhile , " say Harley . " I produce my charge , and then I leave 
 you to dispute the only witness that I bring . Audley Egerton , you take 
 from I the grave trust one man can confide to another . you know how 
 I love Leonora Avenel . I be forbid to see and urge my suit ; you have 
 the access to her presence which be deny to myself . I pray you 
 to remove scruple that I deem too generous , and to woo she not to 
 dishonour , but to be my wife . be it so ? answer . " 

 " it be true , " say Audley , his hand clench at his heart . " you see she 
 whom I thus love , - she thus confide to your honour . you woo she for 
 yourself . be it so ? " 

 " Harley , I deny it not . cease here . I accept the penalty ; I resign your 
 friendship ; I quit your roof ; I submit to your contempt ; I dare not 
 implore your pardon . cease ; let I go hence , and soon ! " 

 the strong man gasp for breath . Harley look at he steadfastly , then 
 turn away his eye , and go on . " Nay , " say he , " be that ALL ? you 
 woo she for yourself , - you win she . account to I for that life which 
 you wrench from mine . you be silent . I will take on myself your task ; 
 you take that life and destroy it . " 

 " spare I , spare I ! " 

 " what be the fate of she who seem so fresh from heaven when these 
 eye beheld she last ? a broken heart , a dishonour name , an early doom , 
 a forget gravestone ! " 

 " no , no - forget , - no ! " 

 " not forget ! Scarce a year pass , and you be married to another . 
 I aid you to form those nuptial which secure your fortune . you 
 have have rank and power and fame . peer call you the type of english 
 gentleman ; priest hold you as a model of christian honour . strip the 
 mask , Audley Egerton ; let the world know you for what you be ! " 

 Egerton raise his head , and fold his arm calmly ; but he say , with a 
 melancholy humility , " I bear all from you ; it be just . say on . " 

 " you take from I the heart of Nora Avenel . you abandon she , you 
 destroy . and her memory cast no shadow over your daily sunshine ; while 
 over my thought , over my life - oh , Egerton - Audley , Audley - how could 
 you have deceive I thus ! " here the inherent tenderness under all this 
 hate , the fount imbed under the harden stone , break out . Harley 
 be ashamed of his weakness , and hurry on , 

 " deceive , - not for an hour , a day , but through blighted youth , through 
 listless manhood , - you suffer I to nurse the remorse that should have 
 be your own ; her life slain , mine waste , - and shall neither of we 
 have revenge ? " 

 " revenge ! ah , Harley , you have have it ! " 

 " no , but I await it ! not in vain from the charnel have come to I the 
 record I produce . and whom do fate select to discover the wrong of 
 the mother , whom appoint as her avenger ? your son , - your own son ; your 
 abandon , nameless son ! " 

 " Son ! son ! " 

 " whom I deliver from famine , or from bad ; and who , in return , have 
 give into my hand the evidence which proclaim in you the perjure 
 friend of Harley L'Estrange , and the fraudulent seducer , under mock 
 marriage form - bad than all franker sin - of Leonora Avenel . " 

 " it be false ! false ! " exclaim Egerton , all his stateliness and all his 
 energy restore to he . " I forbid you to speak thus to I . I forbid you 
 by one word to sully the memory of my lawful wife ! " 

 " ah ! " say Harley , startle . " ah ! false ? prove that , and revenge be 
 over ! thank Heaven ! " 

 " prove it ! what so easy ? and wherefore have I delay the proof ; 
 wherefore conceal , but from tenderness to you , - dread , too - a selfish 
 but human dread - to lose in you the sole esteem that I covet ; the only 
 mourner who would have shed one tear over the stone inscribe with some 
 lie epitaph , in which it will suit a party purpose to proclaim the 
 gratitude of a nation . vain hope . I resign it ! but you speak of a son . 
 Alas , alas ! you be again deceive . I hear that I have a son , - year , 
 long year ago . I seek he , and find a grave . but bless you , Harley , 
 if you succour one whom you even erringly suspect to be Leonora 's 
 child ! " he stretch forth his hand as he speak . 

 " of your son we will speak later , " say Harley , strangely soften . " but 
 before I say more of he , let I ask you to explain ; let I hope that 
 you can extenuate what - " 

 " you be right , " interrupt Egerton , with eager quickness . " you would 
 know from my own lip at last the plain tale of my own offence against 
 you . it be due to both . patiently hear I out . " 

 then Egerton tell all , - his own love for Nora , his struggle against 
 what he feel as treason to his friend , his sudden discovery of Nora 's 
 love for he ; on that discovery , the overthrow of all his resolution ; 
 their secret marriage , their separation ; Nora 's flight , to which Audley 
 still assign but her groundless vague suspicion that their nuptial 
 have not be legal , and her impatience of his own delay in acknowledge 
 the rite . 

 his listener interrupt he here with a few question , the clear 
 and prompt reply to which enable Harley to detect Levy 's plausible 
 perversion of the fact ; and he vaguely guess the cause of the 
 usurer 's falsehood , in the criminal passion which the ill - fat bride 
 have inspire . 

 " Egerton , " say Harley , stifle with an effort his own wrath against 
 the vile deceiver both of wife and husband , " if , on read those 
 paper , you find that Leonora have more excuse for her suspicion and 
 flight than you now deem , and discover perfidy in one to whom you 
 trust your secret , leave his punishment to Heaven . all that you say 
 convince I more and more that we can not even see through the cloud , 
 much less guide the thunderbolt . but proceed . " 

 Audley look surprised and startle , and his eye turn wistfully 
 towards the paper ; but after a short pause he continue his recital . he 
 come to Nora 's unexpected return to her father 's house , her death , his 
 conquest of his own grief , that he might spare Harley the abrupt 
 shock of learn her decease . he have tear himself from the dead , in 
 remorseful sympathy with the living . he speak of Harley 's illness , so 
 nearly fatal , repeat Harley 's jealous word , " that he would rather 
 mourn Nora 's death , than take comfort from the thought that she have 
 love another . " he speak of his journey to the village where Mr. Dale 
 have tell he Nora 's child be place - " and , hear that child and 
 mother be alike gone , whom now could I right by acknowledge a bond 
 that I fear would so wring your heart ? " Audley again pause a moment , 
 and resume in short , nervous , impressive sentence . this cold , austere 
 man of the world for the first time bar his heart , - unconscious , 
 perhaps , that he do so ; unconscious that he reveal how deeply , 
 amidst State care and public distinction , he have feel the absence of 
 affection ; how mechanical be that outer circle in the fold of life 
 which be call a " career ; " how valueless wealth have grow - none to 
 inherit it . of his gnawing and progressive disease alone he do not 
 speak ; he be too proud and too masculine to appeal to pity for physical 
 ill . he remind Harley how often , how eagerly , year after year , month 
 after month , he have urge his friend to rouse himself from mournful 
 dream , devote his native power to his country , or seek the surer 
 felicity of domestic tie . " selfish in these attempt I might be , " say 
 Egerton ; " it be only if I see you restore to happiness that I could 
 believe you could calmly hear my explanation of the past , and on the 
 floor of some happy home grant I your forgiveness . I long to confess , 
 and I dare not . often have the word rush to my lip , - as often 
 some chance sentence from you repel I . in a word , with you be so 
 entwine all the thought and affection of my youth - even those 
 that haunt the grave of Nora - that I could not bear to resign your 
 friendship , and , surround by the esteem and honour of a world I care 
 not for , to meet the contempt of your reproachful eye . " 

 amidst all that Audley say , amidst all that admit of no excuse , 
 two predominant sentiment stand clear , in unmistakable and touching 
 pathos , - remorseful regret for the lost Nora , and self - accuse , 
 earnest , almost feminine tenderness for the friend he have deceive . 
 thus , as he continue to speak , Harley more and more forgot even the 
 remembrance of his own guilty and terrible interval of hate ; the gulf 
 that have so darkly yawn between the two close up , leave they still 
 stand side by side , as in their schoolboy day . but he remain 
 silent , listen , shade his face from Audley , and as if under some 
 soft but enthral spell , till Egerton thus close , 

 " and now , Harley , all be tell . you speak of revenge ? " 

 " revenge ! " mutter Harley , start . 

 " and believe I , " continue Egerton , " be revenge in your power , I 
 should rejoice at it as an atonement . to receive an injury in return 
 for that which , first from youthful passion , and afterwards from the 
 infirmity of purpose that conceal the wrong , I have inflict 
 upon you - why , that would soothe my conscience , and raise my lost 
 self - esteem . the sole revenge you can bestow take the form which most 
 humiliate I , - to revenge be to pardon . " 

 Harley groan ; and still hide his face with one hand , stretch forth 
 the other , but rather with the air of one who entreat than who accord 
 forgiveness . Audley take and press the hand thus extend . 

 " and now , Harley , farewell . with the dawn I leave this house . I 
 can not now accept your aid in this election . Levy shall announce my 
 resignation . randal Leslie , if you so please it , may be return in 
 my stead . he have ability which , under safe guidance , may serve his 
 country ; and I have no right to reject from vain pride whatever will 
 promote the career of one whom I undertake , and have fail , to serve . " 

 " ay , ay , " mutter Harley ; " think not of randal Leslie ; think but of 
 your son . " 

 " my son ! but be you sure that he still live ? you smile ; you - you - oh , 
 Harley , I take from you the mother , - give to I the son ; break my heart 
 with gratitude . your revenge be find ! " 

 Lord L'Estrange rise with a sudden start , gaze on Audley for a 
 moment , - irresolute , not from resentment , but from shame . at that moment 
 he be the man humble ; he be the man who fear reproach , and who 
 need pardon . Audley , not divine what be thus pass in Harley 's 
 breast , turn away . 

 " you think that I ask too much ; and yet all that I can give to the child 
 of my love and the heir of my name be the worthless blessing of a ruin 
 man . Harley , I say no more . I dare not add , ' you too love his mother ! 
 and with a deep and a nobler love than mine . ' " he stop short , and 
 Harley fling himself on his breast . 

 " Me - I - pardon I , Audley ! your offence have be slight to mine . you 
 have tell I your offence ; never can I name to you my own . rejoice that 
 we have both to exchange forgiveness , and in that exchange we be equal 
 still , Audley , brother still . look up ! look up ! think that we be boy 
 now as we be once , - boy who have have their wild quarrel , and who , the 
 moment it be over , feel dearer to each other than before . " 

 " oh , Harley , this be revenge ! it strike home , " murmur Egerton , and 
 tear gush fast from eye that could have gaze unwinking on the rack . 
 the clock strike ; Harley spring forward . 

 " I have time yet , " he cry . " much to do and to undo . you be save from 
 the grasp of Levy ; your election will be win ; your fortune in much may 
 be restore ; you have before you honour not yet achieve ; your career 
 as yet be scarce begin ; your son will embrace you to - morrow . let I 
 go - your hand again ! ah , Audley , we shall be so happy yet ! " 




 CHAPTER XXXI . 

 " there be a hitch , " say Dick , pithily , when Randal join he in the 
 oak copse at ten o'clock . " life be full of hitch . " 

 RANDAL . - " the art of life be to smooth they away . what hitch be this , my 
 dear avenel ? " 

 DICK . - " Leonard have take huff at certain expression of Lord 
 L'Estrange 's at the nomination to - day , and talk of retire from the 
 contest . " 

 RANDAL   with secret glee . - " but his resignation would smooth a 
 hitch , - not create one . the vote promise to he would thus be free , 
 and go to - " 

 DICK . - " the Right honourable Red - Tapist ! " 

 RANDAL . - " be you serious ? " 

 DICK . - " as an undertaker ! the fact be , there be two party among the 
 yellow as there be in the Church , - High Yellow and Low Yellow . Leonard 
 have make great way with the High Yellows , and have more influence with 
 they than I ; and the high Yellows infinitely prefer Egerton to 
 yourself . they say , ' politic apart , he would be an honour to the 
 borough . ' Leonard be of the same opinion ; and if he retire , I do not 
 think I could coax either he or the highflyer to make you any the 
 well by his resignation . " 

 RANDAL . - " but surely your nephew 's sense of gratitude to you would 
 induce he not to go against your wish ? "

 canvass . " 

 DICK . - " I suspect that the borough - monger have get a seat for Egerton

 prefer I to he , without a pang . "

 DICK . - " and I have two private bill I want to smuggle through

 Public Opinion , unless he want to knock his own head against a stone 
 wall ; and that Public Opinion be decidedly yellow . " 

 RANDAL   with candour . - " I can not deny that Public Opinion be yellow ; 
 and at my age , it be natural that I should not commit myself to the 
 policy of a former generation . Blue be fast wear out . but , to return

 straight to what I understand to be his agreement with yourself . surely

 " you will not forget that my expense be to be pay . point of honour .

 friend , I rely on you . "

 DICK . - " very true . he must stand . I have great faith in Parson Dale . " 

 Randal glide back through the park . when he come on the terrace , he 
 suddenly encounter Lord L'Estrange . " I have just be privately into

 be so annoyed by some remark in your Lordship 's admirable speech , that 
 he talk of retire from the contest . that would give a new feature

 LORD L'ESTRANGE . - " so Mr. Fairfield would retire in consequence of my 
 remark ! I be go into the town , and I intend to apologize for those 
 remark , and retract they . " 

 RANDAL   joyously . - " noble ! " 

 Lord L'Estrange look at Leslie 's face , upon which the star gleam 
 palely . " Mr. Egerton have think more of your success than of his own , "

 Randal continue on the terrace . perhaps Harley 's last word give he 
 a twinge of compunction . his head sink musingly on his breast , and he 
 pace to and fro the long gravel - walk , summon up all his intellect to 
 resist every temptation to what could injure his self - interest . 

 " skulk knave ! " mutter Harley . " at least there will be nothing to

 be a fair retribution . besides , how else can I deliver Violante ? " 

 he laugh gayly , his heart be so light ; and his foot bound on as 
 fleet as the deer that he startle amongst the fern . 

 a few yard from the turnstile he overtake Richard Avenel , disguise in 
 a rough great - coat and spectacle . nevertheless , Harley 's eye detect 
 the yellow candidate at the first glance . he catch Dick familiarly by

 " on the term I mention to your Lordship ? " say Dick , startle . " I 
 will agree to return one of your candidate ; but it must not be Audley 
 Egerton . " Harley whisper close in Avenel 's ear . 

 Avenel utter an exclamation of amazement . the two gentleman walk on 
 rapidly , and converse with great eagerness . 

 " certainly , " say Avenel , at length , stop short , " one would do a 
 great deal to serve a family connection , - and a connection that do

 brother - in - law , - a gentleman of such high standing , pull up the whole 
 family ! how pleased Mrs. Richard Avenel will be ! why the devil do not 
 I know it before ? and poor - dear - dear Nora . ah , that she be live ! " 
 Dick 's voice tremble . 

 " her name will be right ; and I will explain why it be my fault that 
 Egerton do not before acknowledge his marriage , and claim you as a 
 brother . come , then , it be all fix and settle . " 

 " no , my Lord ; I be pledge the other way . I do not   see how I can get

 Quixotic ; but still my word be give that if I retire from the election , 
 I will do my good to return Leslie instead of Egerton . " 

 " I know that through Baron Levy . but if your nephew retire ? " 

 " oh , that would solve all difficulty . but the poor boy have now a wish 
 to come into parliament ; and he have do I a service in the hour of 
 need . " 

 " leave it to I . and as to Randal Leslie , he shall have an occasion 
 himself to acquit you and redeem himself ; and happy , indeed , will it 
 be for he if he have yet one spark of gratitude , or one particle of

 the two continue to converse for a few moment , Dick seem to forget 
 the election itself , and ask question of more interest to his heart , 
 which Harley answer so , that Dick wring L'Estrange 's hand with great 
 emotion , and mutter , " my poor mother ! I understand now why she would 
 never talk to I of Nora . when may I tell she the truth ? " 

 " to - morrow evening , after the election , Egerton shall embrace you all . " 

 Dick start , and say , " see Leonard as soon as you can , - there be
