 now restore to the foot , that seem loath to leave a print upon the 
 mire . 

 at the commencement of the High Street he encounter Mr. Dale and 
 Fairfield , walk slowly , arm - in - arm . 

 HARLEY . - " Leonard , I be come to you . give I your hand . forget for 
 the present the word that justly sting and offend you . I will do more 
 than apologize , - I will repair the wrong . excuse I , Mr. Dale , I have 
 one word to say in private to Leonard . " he draw Fairfield aside . 

 " Avenel tell I that if you be to retire from this contest , it would 
 be a sacrifice of inclination . be it so ? " 

 " my Lord , I have sorrow that I would fain forget ; and though I at first 
 shrunk from the strife in which I have be since engage , yet now a 
 literary career seem to I to have lose its old charm ; and I find that , 
 in public life , there be a distraction to the thought which embitter 
 solitude , that book fail to bestow . therefore , if you still wish I to 
 continue this contest , though I know not your motive , it will not be 
 as it be to begin it , - a reluctant and a painful obedience to your 
 request . " 

 " I understand . it be a sacrifice of inclination to begin the contest ; 
 it would be now a sacrifice of inclination to withdraw ? " 

 " honestly , yes , my Lord . " 

 " I rejoice to hear it , for I ask that sacrifice , - a sacrifice which you 
 will recall hereafter with delight and pride ; a sacrifice sweeter , if 
 I read your nature aright - oh , sweet far , than all which commonplace 
 ambition could bestow ! and when you learn why I make this demand , you 
 will say , ' this , indeed , be reparation for the word that wound my 
 affection , and wrong my heart . ' " 

 " my Lord , my Lord ! " exclaim Leonard , " the injury be repair already . 
 you give I back your esteem , when you so well anticipate my answer . 
 your esteem ! - life smile again . I can return to my more legitimate 
 career without a sigh . I have no need of distraction from thought 
 now . you will believe that , whatever my past presumption , I can pray 
 sincerely for your happiness . " 

 " Poet , you adorn your career ; you fulfil your mission , even at this 
 moment ; you beautify the world ; you give to the harsh form of Duty 
 the cestus of the Graces , " say Harley , try to force a smile to his 
 quivering lip . " but we must hasten back to the prose of existence . I 
 accept your sacrifice . as for the time and mode I must select in order 
 to insure its result , I will ask you to abide by such instruction as I 
 shall have occasion to convey through your uncle . till then , no word 
 of your intention , - not even to Mr. Dale . forgive I if I would rather 
 secure Mr. Egerton 's election than your . let that explanation suffice 
 for the present . what think you , by the way , of Audley Egerton ? " 

 " I think when I hear he speak and when he close with those touching 
 word , - imply that he leave all of his life not devoted to his country 
 ' to the charity of his friend , ' - how proudly , even as his opponent , I 
 could have clasp his hand ; and if he have wrong I in private life , 
 I should have think it ingratitude to the country he have so serve to 
 remember the offence . " 

 Harley turn away abruptly , and join Mr. Dale . 

 " leave Leonard to go home by himself ; you see that I have heal 
 whatever wound I inflict on he . " 

 parson . - " and , your well nature thus awaken , I trust , my dear Lord , 
 that you have altogether abandon the idea of - " 

 HARLEY . - " revenge ? - no . and if you do not approve that revenge 
 to - morrow , I will never rest till I have see you - a bishop ! " 

 MR . DALE   much shocked . - " my Lord , for shame ! " 

 HARLEY   seriously . - " my levity be but lip - deep , my dear Mr. Dale . but 
 sometimes the froth on the wave show the change in the tide . " 

 the parson look at he earnestly , and then seize he by both hand 
 with holy gladness and affection . 

 " return to the Park now , " say Harley , smile ; " and tell Violante , 
 if it be not too late to see she , that she be even more eloquent than 
 you . " 

 Lord L'Estrange bound forward . 

 Mr. Dale walk back through the park to Lansmere House . on the terrace 
 he find Randal , who be still pace to and fro , sometimes in the 
 starlight , sometimes in the shadow . 

 Leslie look up , and see Mr. Dale , the close astuteness of his 
 aspect return ; and step out of the starlight deep into the shadow , 
 he say , 

 " I be sorry to learn that Mr. Fairfield have be so hurt by Lord 
 L'Estrange 's severe allusion . pity that political difference should 
 interfere with private friendship ; but I hear that you have be to 
 Mr. Fairfield , - and , doubtless , as the peacemaker . perhaps you meet 
 Lord L'Estrange by the way ? he promise I that he would apologize and 
 retract . " 

 " good young man ! " say the unsuspecting parson , " he have do so . " 

 " and Mr. Leonard Fairfield will , therefore , I presume , continue the 
 contest ? " 

 " Contest - ah , this election ! I suppose so , of course . but I grieve that 
 he should stand against you , who seem to be dispose towards he so 
 kindly . " 

 " oh , " say Randal , with a benevolent smile , " we have fight before , you 
 know , and I beat he then . I may do so again ! " 

 and he walk into the house , arm - in - arm with the parson . Mr. Dale 
 seek Violante ; Leslie retire to his own room , and feel his election 
 be secure . 

 Lord L'Estrange have gain the thick of the street - pass group of 
 roar enthusiast - Blue and Yellow - now meet with a cheer , now follow 
 by a groan . just by a public - house that form the angle of a lane with 
 the High Street , and which be all ablaze with light and all alive with 
 clamour , he behold the graceful baron lean against the threshold , 
 smoke his cigar , too refined to associate its divine vapour with the 
 wreath of shag within , and chat agreeably with a knot of female , 
 who be either attract by the general excitement , or wait to see 
 husband , brother , father , or son , who be now join in the chorus 
 of " Blue forever ! " that ring from tap - room to attic of the illumined 
 hostelry . Levy , see Lord L'Estrange , withdraw his cigar from his 
 lip , and hasten to join he . " all the Hundred and Fifty be in 
 there , " say the baron , with a backward significant jerk of his thumb 
 towards the inn . " I have see they all privately , in ten at a time ; 
 and I have be tell the lady without that it will be good for the 
 interest of their family to go home , and let we lock up the Hundred 
 and fifty safe from the Yellows , till we bring they to the poll . but I 
 be afraid , " continue Levy , " that the rascal be not to be rely upon 
 unless I actually pay they beforehand ; and that would be disreputable , 
 immoral , - and , what be more , it would upset the election . besides , if 
 they be pay beforehand , query , be it quite sure how they will vote 
 afterwards ? " 

 " Mr. Avenel , I dare say , can manage they , " say Harley . " pray do nothing 
 immoral , and nothing that will upset the election . I think you might as 
 well go home . " 

 " home ! no , pardon I , my Lord ; there must be some head to direct the 
 Committee , and keep our captain at their post upon the doubtful 
 elector . a great deal of mischief may be do between this and the 
 morrow ; and I would sit up all night - ay , six night a week for the next 
 three month - to prevent any awkward mistake by which Audley Egerton can 
 be return . " 

 " his return would really grieve you so much ? " say Harley . 

 " you may judge of that by the zeal with which I enter into all your 
 design . " 

 here there be a sudden and wondrously loud shout from another inn , - a 
 Yellow inn , far down the lane , not so luminous as the Blue hostelry ; on 
 the contrary , look rather dark and sinister , more like a place for 
 conspirator or felon than honest , independent elector , - " avenel 
 forever ! Avenel and the yellow ! " 

 " excuse I , my Lord , I must go back and watch over my black sheep , if 
 I would have they blue ! " say Levy ; and he retreat towards the 
 threshold . but at that shout of " Avenel forever ! " as if at a signal , 
 various elector of the redoubte Hundred and Fifty rush from the Blue 
 hostelry , sweep past Levy , and hurry down the lane to the dark 
 little Yellow inn , follow by the female straggler , as small bird 
 follow an owl . it be not , however , very easy to get into that Yellow 
 inn ; Yellow Reformers , eminent for their zeal on behalf of purity of 
 election , be station outside the door , and only strain in one 
 candidate for admittance at a time . " after all , " think the baron , as 
 he pass into the principal room of the blue tavern , and propose the 
 national song of " Rule Britannia , " - " after all , Avenel hat Egerton as 
 much as I do , and both side work to the same end . " and thrum on the 
 table , he join with a fine lass in the famous line , 

  " for Britons never will be slave ! " 

 in the interim , Harley have disappear within the Lansmere Arms , which 
 be the headquarters of the Blue Committee . not , however , mount to 
 the room in which a few of the more indefatigable be continue their 
 labour , receive report from scout , give order , lay wager , 
 and very muzzy with british principle and spirit , Harley call aside 
 the landlord , and inquire if the stranger , for whom room have be 
 prepare , be yet arrive . an affirmative answer be give , and Harley 
 follow the host up a private stair , to a part of the house remote from 
 the room devote to the purpose of the election . he remain with this 
 stranger about half an hour , and then walk into the Committee - room , 
 get rid of the more excited , confer with the more sober , issue a few 
 brief direction to such of the leader as he feel he could most rely 
 upon , and return home as rapidly as he have quit it . 

 Dawn be gray in the sky when Harley seek his own chamber . to 
 gain it , he pass by the door of Violante 's . his heart suffuse with 
 grateful ineffable tenderness , he pause and kiss the threshold . when 
 he stand within his room   the same that he have occupy in his early 
 youth , he feel as if the load of year be lift from his bosom . the 
 joyous , divine elasticity of spirit , that in the morning of life spring 
 towards the Future as a bird soar into heaven , pervade his whole sense 
 of be . a greek poet imply that the height of bliss be the sudden 
 relief of pain : there be a nobler bliss still , - the rapture of the 
 conscience at the sudden release from a guilty thought . by the bedside 
 at which he have kneel in boyhood , Harley pause to kneel once more . the 
 luxury of prayer , interrupt since he have nourish scheme of which 
 his passion have blind he to the sin , but which , nevertheless , he 
 dare not confess to the All - Merciful , be restore to he . and yet , 
 as he bow his knee , the elation of spirit he have before feel forsook 
 he . the sense of the danger his soul have escape , the full knowledge of 
 the guilt to which the fiend have tempt , come dread before his clearing 
 vision ; he shudder in horror of himself . and he who but a few hour 
 before have deem it so impossible to pardon his fellow - man , now feel 
 as if year of useful and beneficent deed could alone purify his own 
 repentant soul from the memory of one hateful passion . 




 CHAPTER XXXII 

 but while Harley have thus occupy the hour of night with care for the 
 living , Audley Egerton have be in commune with the dead . he have take 
 from the pile of paper amidst which it have fall , the record of Nora 's 
 silence heart . with a sad wonder he see how he have once be love . 
 what have all which successful ambition have bestow on the lonely 
 statesman to compensate for the glorious empire he have lose , - such 
 realm of lovely fancy ; such world of exquisite emotion ; that infinite 
 which lie within the divine sphere that unite spiritual genius with 
 human love ? his own positive and earthly nature attain , for the 
 first time , and as if for its own punishment , the comprehension of that 
 loftier and more ethereal visitant from the heaven , who have once look 
 with a seraph 's smile through the prison - bar of his iron life ; that 
 celestial refinement of affection , that exuberance of feeling which 
 warm into such variety of beautiful idea , under the breath of the 
 earth - beautifier , Imagination , - all from which , when it be all his own , 
 he have turn half weary and impatient , and term the exaggeration 
 of a visionary romance , now that the world have lose they evermore , he 
 interpret aright as truth . truth they be , although illusion . even 
 as the philosopher tell we that the splendour of colour which deck the 
 universe be not on the surface whereon we think to behold it , but in our 
 own vision ; yet , take the colour from the universe , and what philosophy 
 can assure we that the universe have sustain no loss ? 

 but when Audley come to that passage in the fragment which , though but 
 imperfectly , explain the true cause of Nora 's flight ; when he see how 
 Levy , for what purpose he be unable to conjecture , have suggest to his 
 bride the doubt that have offend he , - assert the marriage to be a 
 fraud , draw from Audley 's own brief resentful letter to Nora proof of 
 the assertion , mislead so naturally the young wife 's scanty experience of 
 actual life , and madden one so sensitively pure into the conviction of 
 dishonour , - his brow darken , and his hand clench . he rise and go 
 at once to Levy 's room . he find it desert , inquire , learn that 
 Levy be go forth , and have leave word he might not be at home for the 
 night . Fortunate , perhaps , for Audley , fortunate for the baron , that 
 they do not then meet . revenge , in spite of his friend 's admonition , 
 might at that hour have be as potent an influence on Egerton as it have 
 be on Harley , and not , as with the latter , to be turn aside . 

 Audley come back to his room and finish the tragic record . he trace 
 the tremor of that beloved hand through the last torture of doubt and 
 despair ; he see where the hot tear have fall ; he see where the hand 
 have pause , the very sentence not conclude ; mentally he accompany 
 his - fated bride in the dismal journey to her maiden home , and beheld 
 she before he as he have last see , more beautiful even in death than 
 the face of live woman have ever since appear to he ; and as he bend 
 over the last word , the blank that they leave on the leaf , stretch 
 pale beyond the quiver of the character and the blister of the 
 tear , - pale and blank as the void which depart love leave behind 
 it , - he feel his heart suddenly stand still , its course arrest as the 
 record close . it beat again , but feebly , - so feebly ! his breath become 
 labour and pain , his sight grow dizzy ; but the constitutional firmness 
 and fortitude of the man clung to he in the stubborn mechanism of 
 habit , his will yet fight against his disease , life rally as the 
 light flicker up in the wan taper . 

 the next morning , when Harley come into his friend 's room , Egerton be 
 asleep . but the sleep seem much disturb ; the breathing be hard 
 and difficult ; the bed - clothe be partially throw off , as if in the 
 tossing of disturb dream ; the sinewy strong arm , the broad athletic 
 breast , be partly bare . strange that so deadly a disease within should 
 leave the frame such apparent power that , to the ordinary eye , the 
 sleeping sufferer seem a model of healthful vigour . one hand be 
 thrust with uneasy straining over the pillow , - it have its hold on 
 the fatal paper ; a portion of the leaf be visible ; and where the 
 character have be blur by Nora 's tear , be the trace , yet moist , 
 of tear perhaps more bitter . 

 Harley feel deeply affected ; and while he still stand by the bed , 
 Egerton sigh heavily and wake . he star round he , as if perplex 
 and confuse , till his eye rest on Harley , he smile and say , 

 " so early ! ah , I remember , it be the day for our great boat - race . we 
 shall have the current against we ; but you and I together - when do we 
 ever lose ? " 

 Audley 's mind be wander ; it have go back to the old Eton day . but 
 Harley think that he speak in metaphorical allusion to the present 
 more important contest . 

 " true , my Audley , - you and I together - when do we ever lose ? but will 
 you rise ? I wish you would be at the polling - place to shake hand with 
 your voter as they come up . by four o'clock you will be release , and 
 the election won . " 

 " the election ! how ! what ! " say Egerton , recover himself . " I 
 recollect now . yes , - I accept this last kindness from you . I always say 
 I would die in harness . public life - I have no other . ah , I dream again ! 
 oh , Harley my son , my son ! " 

 " you shall see he after four o'clock . you will be proud of each other . 
 but make haste and dress . shall I ring the bell for your servant ? " 

 " do , " say Egerton , briefly , and sink back . Harley quit the room , 
 and join Randal and some of the more important member of the Blue 
 Committee , who be already hurry over their breakfast . 

 all be anxious and nervous except Harley , who dip his dry toast 
 into his coffee , accord to his ordinary abstemious italian habit , 
 with serene composure . randal in vain try for an equal tranquillity . 
 but though sure of his election , there would necessarily follow a scene 
 try to the nerve of his hypocrisy . he would have to affect profound 
 chagrin in the midst of vile joy ; have to act the part of decorous 
 high - minded sorrow , that by some untoward chance , some unaccountable 
 cross - splitting , Randal Leslie 's gain should be Audley Egerton 's loss . 
 besides , he be flurry in the expectation of see the squire , and 
 of appropriate the money which be to secure the dear object of his 
 ambition . Breakfast be soon despatch . the committee - man , bustle for 
 their hat , and look at their watch , give the signal for departure ; 
 yet no Squire Hazeldean have make his appearance . Harley , step from

 follow . 

 " Mr. Leslie , " say Harley , lean against the balustrade , and 
 carelessly pat Nero 's rough , honest head , " you remember that 
 you be good enough to volunteer to I the explanation of certain 
 circumstance in connection with the Count di Peschiera , which you give 
 to the Duke di Serrano ; and I reply that my thought be at present 
 engage on the election , but as soon as that be over , I should be very 
 willing to listen to any communication affect yourself and my old 
 friend the duke , with which you might be pleased to favour I . " 

 this address take Randal by surprise , and do not tend to calm his 
 nerve . however , he reply readily , 

 " upon that , as upon any other matter that may influence the judgment you 
 form of I , I shall be but too eager to remove a single doubt that , in 
 your eye , can rest upon my honour . " 

 " you speak exceedingly well , Mr. Leslie ; no man can express himself more 
 handsomely ; and I will claim your promise with the less scruple because 
 the duke be powerfully affect by the reluctance of his daughter 
 to ratify the engagement that bind his honour , in case your own be 
 indisputably clear . I may boast of some influence over the young lady , 
 since I assist to save she from the infamous plot of Peschiera ; and 
 the duke urge I to receive your explanation , in the belief that , if 
 it satisfy I , as it have satisfy he , I may conciliate his child in 
 favour of the address of a suitor who would have hazard his very 
 life against so redoubte a duellist as Peschiera . " 

 " Lord L'Estrange , " reply randal , bow , " I shall indeed owe you much 
 if you can remove that reluctance on the part of my betrothed bride , 
 which alone cloud my happiness , and which would at once put an end to 
 my suit , do I not ascribe it to an imperfect knowledge of myself , which 
 I shall devote my life to improve into confidence and affection . " 

 " no man can speak more handsomely , " reiterate Harley , as if with 
 profound admiration ; and indeed he do eye Randal as we eye some rare 
 curiosity . " I be happy to inform you , too , " continue L'Estrange , " that 
 if your marriage with the Duke of Serrano 's daughter take place - " 

 " if ! " echo Randal . 

 " I beg pardon for make an hypothesis of what you claim the right to 
 esteem a certainty , - I correct my expression : when your marriage with 
 that young lady take place , you will at least escape the rock on which 
 many young man of ardent affection have split at the onset of the grand 
 voyage . you will form no imprudent connection . in a word , I receive 
 yesterday a despatch from Vienna , which contain the full pardon and 
 formal restoration of Alphonso , Duke di Serrano . and I may add , that the 
 austrian government   sometimes misunderstood in this country   be bind 
 by the law it administer , and can in no way dictate to the duke , once 
 restore , as to the choice of his son - in - law , or as to the heritage that 
 may devolve on his child . " 

 " and do the duke yet know of his recall ? " exclaim Randal , his cheek 
 flush and his eye sparkle . 

 " no . I reserve that good news , with other matter , till after the 
 election be over . but Egerton keep we wait sadly . ah , here come his 
 valet . " 

 Audley 's servant approach . " Mr. Egerton feel himself rather more 
 poorly than usual , my Lord ; he beg you will excuse his go with 
 you into the town at present . he will come later if his presence be 
 absolutely necessary . " 

 " no . pray tell he to rest and nurse himself . I should have like he to 
 witness his own triumph , - that be all . say I will represent he at the 
 polling - place . gentleman , be you ready ? we will go on . " 

 the polling booth be erect in the centre of the marketplace . the 
 voting have already commence ; and Mr. Avenel and Leonard be already at 
 their post , in order to salute and thank the voter in their cause who 
 pass before they . randal and L'Estrange enter the booth amidst loud 
 hurrah , and to the national air of " see the Conquering Hero come . " the 
 voter defile in quick succession . those who vote entirely accord 
 to principle or colour - which come to much the same thing - and be 
 therefore above what be term " management , " flock in first , vote 
 straightforwardly for both blue or both yellow . at the end of the 
 first half - hour the yellow be about ten ahead of the Blues . then 
 sundry split vote begin to perplex conjecture as to the result ; and 
 Randal , at the end of the first hour , have fifteen majority over Audley 
 Egerton , two over Dick Avenel , Leonard Fairfield head the poll by 
 five . randal owe his place in the list to the voter that Harley 's 
 personal effort have procure for he ; and he be well pleased to see 
 that Lord L'Estrange have not withdraw from he a single promise so 
 obtain . this augur well for Harley 's ready belief in his appoint 
 " explanation . " in short , the whole election seem go just as he 
 have calculate . but by twelve o'clock there be some change in the 
 relative position of the candidate . Dick Avenel have gradually gain 
 ground , - pass Randal , pass even Leonard . he stand at the head 
 of the poll by a majority of ten . Randal come next . Audley be twenty 
 behind Randal , and Leonard four behind Audley . More than half the 
 constituency have poll , but none of the Committee on either side , nor 
 one of the redoubte corps of a hundred and fifty . 

 the poll now slacken sensibly . randal , look round , and long for 
 an opportunity to ask Dick whether he really mean to return himself 
 instead of his nephew , see that Harley have disappear ; and presently a 
 note be bring to he request his presence in the Committee - room . 
 Thither he hasten . 

 as he force his way through the bystander in the lobby , towards the 
 threshold of the room , Levy catch hold of he and whisper , " they 
 begin to fear for Egerton . they want a compromise in order to secure 
 he . they will propose to you to resign , if Avenel will withdraw 
 Leonard . do not   be entrap . L'Estrange may put the question to you ; 
 but - a word in your ear - he would be glad enough to throw over Egerton . 
 rely upon this , and stand firm . " 

 Randal make no answer , but , the crowd give way for he , enter the 
 room . Levy follow . the door be instantly close . all the Blue 
 Committee be assemble . they look heated , anxious , eager . Lord 
 L'Estrange , alone calm and cool , stand at the head of the long table . 
 despite his composure , Harley 's brow be thoughtful . " yes , " say he 
 to himself , " I will give this young man the fair occasion to prove 
 gratitude to his benefactor ; and if he here acquit himself , I will spare 
 he , at least , public exposure of his deceit to other . so young , he 
 must have some good in he , - at least towards the man to whom he owe 
 all . " 

 " Mr. Leslie , " say L'Estrange , aloud , " you see the state of the poll . 
 our Committee believe that , if you continue to stand , Egerton must be 
 beat . they fear that , Leonard Fairfield have little chance , the 
 yellow will not waste their second vote on he , but will transfer they 
 to you , in order to keep out Egerton . if you retire , Egerton will be 
 safe . there be reason to suppose that Leonard would , in that case , also 
 be withdraw . " 

 " you can hope and fear nothing more from Egerton , " whisper Levy . " he 
 be utterly ruin ; and , if he lose , will sleep in a prison . the bailiff 
 be wait for he . " 

 Randal be still silent , and at that silence an indignant murmur run 
 through the more influential member of the Committee . for , though 
 Audley be not personally very popular , still a candidate so eminent be 
 necessarily their first object , and they would seem very small to 
 the Yellows , if their great man be defeat by the very candidate 
 introduce to aid he , - a youth unknown . vanity and patriotism 
 both swell that murmur . " you see , young sir , " cry a rich , blunt 
 master - butcher , " that it be an honourable understanding that Mr. 
 Egerton be to be safe . you have no claim on we , except as fight 
 second to he . and we be all astonish that you do not   say at once , 
 ' Save Egerton , of course . ' excuse my freedom , sir . no time for palaver . " 

 " Lord L'Estrange , " say Randal , turn mildly from the butcher , " do 
 you , as the first here in rank and influence , and as Mr. Egerton 's 
 especial friend , call upon I to sacrifice my election , and what appear 
 to be the inclination of the majority of the constituent , in order to 
 obtain what be , after all , a doubtful chance of return Mr. Egerton in 
 my room ? 

 " I do not call upon you , Mr. Leslie . it be a matter of feeling or of 
 honour , which a gentleman can very well decide for himself . " 

 " be any such compact made between your Lordship and myself , when you 
 first give I your interest and canvass for I in person ? " 

 " certainly not . gentleman , be silent . no such compact be mention by 
 I . " 

 " neither be it by Mr. Egerton . whatever might be the understanding 
 speak of by the respected elector who address I , I be no party to 
 it . I be persuade that Mr. Egerton be the last person who would wish 
 to owe his election to a trick upon the elector in the midst of 
 the polling , and to what the world would consider a very unhandsome 
 treatment of myself , upon whom all the toil of the canvass have 
 devolve . " 

 again the murmur rise ; but Randal have an air so determined , that it 
 quell resentment , and obtain a continue , though most chilling and 
 half - contemptuous hearing . 

 " nevertheless , " resume Randal , " I would at once retire be I not under 
 the firm persuasion that I shall convince all present , who now seem to 
 condemn I , that I act precisely accord to Mr. Egerton 's own private 
 inclination . that gentleman , in fact , have never be amongst you , have 
 not canvass in person , have take no trouble , beyond a speech , that 
 be evidently mean to be but a general defence of his past political 
 career . what do this mean ? simply that his standing have be merely a 
 form , to comply with the wish of his party , against his own desire . " 

 the committee - man look at each other amazed and doubtful . randal saw 
 he have gain an advantage ; he pursue it with a tact and ability which 
 show that , in spite of his mere oratorical deficiency , he have in 
 he the element of a dexterous debater . " I will be plain with you , 
 gentleman . my character , my desire to stand well with you all , oblige I 
 to be so . Mr. Egerton do not wish to come into parliament at present . 
 his health be much break ; his private affair need all his time and 
 attention . I be , I may say , as a son to he . he be most anxious for 
 my success ; Lord L'Estrange tell I but last night , very truly , ' more 
 anxious for my success than his own . ' nothing could please he more 
 than to think I be serve in parliament , however humbly , those great 
 interest which neither health nor leisure will , in this momentous 
 crisis , allow himself to defend with his wonted energy . later , 
 indeed , no doubt , he will seek to return to an arena in which he be 
 so distinguished ; and when the popular excitement , which produce the 
 popular injustice of the day , be over , what constituency will not be 
 proud to return such a man ? in support and proof of what I have thus 
 say , I now appeal to Mr. Egerton 's own agent , - a gentleman who , 
 in spite of his vast fortune and the rank he hold in society , have 
 consent to act gratuitously on behalf of that great statesman . I ask 
 you , then , respectfully , Baron Levy , be not Mr. Egerton 's health much 
 break , and in need of rest ? " 

 " it be , " say Levy . 

 " and do not his affair necessitate his serious and undivided 
 attention ? " 

 " they do indeed , " quoth the baron . " gentleman , I have nothing to urge 
 in behalf of my distinguished friend as against the statement of his 
 adopt son , Mr. Leslie . " 

 " then all I can say , " cry the butcher , strike his huge fist on the 
 table , " be , that Mr. Egerton have behave d - -d unhandsome to we , and we 
 shall be the laugh - stock of the borough . " 

 " softly , softly , " say Harley . " there be a knock at the door behind . 
 excuse I . " 

 Harley quit the room , but only for a minute or two . on his return he 
 address himself to Randal . 

 " be we then to understand , Mr. Leslie , that your intention be not to 
 resign ? " 

 " unless your lordship actually urge I to the contrary , I should say , 
 let the election go on , and all take our chance . that seem to I the 
 fair , manly , ENGLISH   great emphasis on the last adjective , honourable 
 course . " 

 " be it so , " reply Harley ; " ' let all take their chance . ' Mr. Leslie , 
 we will no long detain you . go back to the polling - place , - one of the 
 candidate should be present ; and you , Baron Levy , be good enough to go 
 also , and return thank to those who may yet vote for Mr. Egerton . " 

 Levy bow , and go out arm - in - arm with Randal . " Capital , capital , " 
 say the baron . " you have a wonderful head . " 

 " I do not like L'Estrange 's look , nevertheless . but he can not   hurt I 
 now ; the vote he get for I instead of for Egerton have already poll . 
 the Committee , indeed , may refuse to vote for I ; but then there be 
 Avenel 's body of reserve . yes , the election be virtually over . when we 
 get back , Hazeldean will have arrive with the money for the purchase of 
 my ancestral property ; Dr. Riccabocca be already restore to the estate 
 and title of Serrano ; what do I care far for Lord L'Estrange ? 
 still , I do not like his look . " 

 " Pooh , you have do just what he wish . I be forbid to say more . 
 here we be at the booth . a new placard since we leave . how be the 
 number ? avenel forty ahead of you ; you thirty above Egerton ; and 
 Leonard Fairfield still last on the poll . but where be Avenel and 
 Fairfield ? " both those candidate have disappear , perhaps go to their 
 own Committee - room . 

 meanwhile , as soon as the door have close on Randal and the baron , 
 in the midst of the angry hubbub succeed to their departure , Lord 
 L'Estrange spring upon the table . the action and his look still every 
 sound . 

 " gentleman , it be in our hand to return one of our candidate , and to 
 make our own choice between the two . you have hear Mr. Leslie and Baron 
 Levy . to their statement I make but this reply , - Mr. Egerton be need 
 by the country ; and whatever his health or his affair , he be ready to 
 respond to that call . if he have not canvass , if he do not appear 
 before you at this moment , the service of more than twenty year plead 
 for he in his stead . which , then , of the two candidate do you choose 
 as your member , - a renowned statesman , or a beardless boy ? both have 
 ambition and ability ; the one have identify those quality with the 
 history of a country , and   as it be now allege to his prejudice   with 
 a devotion that have break a vigorous frame and injure a princely 
 fortune . the other evince his ambition by invite you to prefer he 
 to his benefactor , and prove his ability by the excuse he make for 
 ingratitude . choose between the two , - an Egerton or a Leslie . " 

 " Egerton forever ! " cry all the assembly , as with a single voice , 
 follow by a hiss for Leslie . 

 " but , " say a grave and prudent Committee - man , " have we really the 
 choice ? do not that rest with the yellow ? be not your Lordship too 
 sanguine ? " 

 " open that door behind ; a deputation from our opponent wait in the 
 room on the other side the passage . admit they . " 

 the Committee be hush in breathless silence while Harley 's order be 
 obey . and soon , to their great surprise , Leonard Fairfield himself , 
 attend by six of the principal member of the Yellow party , enter 
 the room . 

 LORD L'ESTRANGE . - " you have a proposition to make to we , Mr. Fairfield , 
 on behalf of yourself and Mr. Avenel , and with the approval of your 
 Committee ? " 

 LEONARD   advance to the table . - " I have . we be convinced that 
 neither party can carry both its candidate . Mr. Avenel be safe . the 
 only question be , which of the two candidate on your side it well 
 become the honour of this constituency to select . my resignation , which 
 I be about to tender , will free sufficient vote to give the triumph 
 either to Mr. Egerton or to Mr. Leslie . " 

 " Egerton forever ! " cry once more the excited blue . " yes , Egerton 
 forever ! " say Leonard , with a glow upon his cheek . " we may differ from 
 his politic , but who can tell we those of Mr. Leslie ? we may differ 
 from the politician , but who would not feel proud of the senator ? a 
 great and incalculable advantage be bestow on that constituency which 
 return to parliament a distinguished man . his distinction ennoble the 
 place he represent , it sustain public spirit , it augment the manly 
 interest in all that affect the nation . every time his voice hush the 
 assemble parliament , it remind we of our common country ; and even the 
 discussion amongst his constituent which his voice provoke , clear 
 their perception of the public interest , and enlighten themselves , 
 from the intellect which command their interest , and compel their 
 attention . Egerton , then , forever ! if our party must subscribe to the 
 return of one opponent , let all unite to select the worthy . my 
 Lord L'Estrange , when I quit this room , it will be to announce my 
 resignation , and to solicit those who have promise I their vote to 
 transfer they to Mr. Audley Egerton . " 

 amidst the uproarious huzzas which follow this speech , Leonard draw 
 near to Harley . " my Lord , I have obey your wish , as convey to I 
 by my uncle , who be engage at this moment elsewhere in carry they 
 into effect . " 

 " Leonard , " say Harley , in the same undertone , " you have insure to 
 Audley Egerton what you alone could do , - the triumph over a perfidious 
 dependent , the continuance of the sole career in which he have hitherto 
 find the solace or the zest of life . he must thank you with his own 
 lip . come to the Park after the close of the poll . there and then shall 
 the explanation yet needful to both be give and receive . " 

 here Harley bow to the assembly and raise his voice : " gentleman , 
 yesterday , at the nomination of the candidate , I utter remark that 
 have justly pain Mr. Fairfield . in your presence I wholly retract and 
 frankly apologize for they . in your presence I entreat his forgiveness , 
 and say , that if he will accord I his friendship , I will place he 
 in my esteem and affection side by side with the statesman whom he have 
 give to his country . " 

 Leonard grasp the hand extend to he with both his own , and then , 
 overcome by his emotion , hurry from the room ; while Blues and yellow 
 exchange greeting , rejoice in the compromise that would dispel all 
 party irritation , secure the peace of the borough , and allow quiet 
 man , who have detest each other the day before , and vow reciprocal 
 injury to trade and custom , the indulgence of all amiable and 
 fraternal feeling - until the next general election . 

 in the mean while the polling have go on slowly as before , but still to 
 the advantage of Randal . " not two - third of the constituency will poll , " 
 murmur Levy , look at his watch . " the thing be decide . Aha , Audley 
 Egerton ! you who once torture I with the unspeakable jealousy that 
 bequeath such implacable hate ; you who scorn my society , and call 
 I ' scoundrel , ' disdainful of the very power your folly place within my 
 hand , - aha , your time be up ! and the spirit that administer to your 
 own destruction stride within the circle to seize its prey ! " 

 " you shall have my first frank , Levy , " say Randal , " to enclose your 
 letter to Mr. Thornhill 's solicitor . this affair of the election be 
 over ; we must now look to what else rest on our hand . " 

 " what the devil be that placard ? " cry Levy , turn pale . 

 randal look , and right up the market - place , follow by an immense 
 throng , move , high over the head of all , a Yellow Board , that seem 
 march through the air , cometlike : - 

  two o'clock p.m.

  avenel and EGERTON . 

   sign   Timothy Alljack 

  Yellow Committee Room . 

 " what infernal treachery be this ? " cry randal , livid with honest 
 indignation . 

 " wait a moment ; there be Avenel ! " exclaim Levy ; and at the head of 
 another procession that emerge from the obscurer lane of the town , 
 walk , with grave majesty , the survive yellow candidate . Dick 
 disappear for a moment within a grocer 's shop in the broad part of 
 the place , and then culminate at the height of a balcony on the first 
 story , just above an enormous yellow canister , significant of the 
 profession and the politic of the householder . no soon do Dick , hat 
 in hand , appear on this rostrum , than the two procession halt below , 
 band cease , flag droop round their stave , crowd rush within 
 hearing , and even the poll clerk spring from the booth . randal and Levy 
 themselves press into the throng . Dick on the balcony be the Deus ex 
 machina . 

 " Freemen and elector ! " say Dick , with his most sonorous accent , 
 " find that the public opinion of this independent and enlighten 
 constituency be so evenly divide , that only one yellow candidate can be 
 return , and only one Blue have a chance , it be my intention last night 
 to retire from the contest , and thus put an end to all bickering and 
 ill - blood   hold your tongue there , can not   you ! . I say honestly , I 
 should have prefer the return of my distinguished and talented young 
 nephew - honourable relation - to my own ; but he would not hear of it , and 
 talk all our Committee into the erroneous but high - minded notion , that 
 the town would cry shame if the nephew ride into parliament by break 
 the back of the uncle . "   loud cheer from the mob , and partial cry of 
 " we will have you both ! " 

 " you will do no such thing , and you know it ; hold your jaw , " resume Dick , 
 with imperious good - humour . " let I go on , can not   you ? - time press . in 
 a word , my nephew resolve to retire , if , at two o'clock this day , there 
 be no chance of return both of we ; and there be none . now , then , the 
 next thing for the yellow who have not yet vote , be to consider how 
 they will give their second vote . if I have be the man to retire , 
 why , for certain reason , I should have recommend they to split with 
 Leslie , - a clever chap , and pretty considerable sharp . " 

 " hear , hear , hear ! " cry the baron , lustily . 

 " but I be bind to say that my nephew have an opinion of his own , - as an 
 independent Britisher , let he be twice your nephew , ought to have ; and 
 his opinion go the other way , and so do that of our Committee . " 

 " sell ! " cry the baron ; and some of the crowd shake their head , and 
 look grave , - especially those suspect of a wish to be buy . 

 " sell ! pretty fellow you with the nosegay in your buttonhole to talk of 
 sell ! you who want to sell your own client , - and you know it .   Levy 
 recoil .   why , gentleman , that be Levy the Jew , who talk of sell ! 
 and if he asperse the character of this constituency , I stand here to 
 defend it ! and there stand the parish pump , with a handle for the arm 
 of Honesty , and a spout for the lip of Falsehood ! " 

 at the close of this magniloquent period , borrow , no doubt , from some 
 great american orator , Baron Levy involuntarily retreat towards the 
 shelter of the polling - booth , follow by some frown yellow with 
 very menacing gesture . 

 " but the calumniator sneak away ; leave he to the reproach of his 
 conscience , " resume Dick , with a generous magnanimity . 

 " SOLD !   the word ring through the place like the blast of a trumpet 
 sell ! no , believe I , not a man who vote for Egerton instead 
 of Fairfield will , so far as I be concern , be a penny the 
 well -   chilling silence   - or   with a scarce perceivable wink 
 towards the anxious face of the Hundred and Fifty who fill the 
 background   - or a penny the bad .   loud cheer from the hundred and 
 Fifty , and cry of ' noble ! '   I do not   like the politic of Mr. Egerton . 
 but I be not only a politician , - I be a MAN ! the argument of our 
 respected Committee - person in business , tender husband , and devoted 
 father - have weight with I . I myself be a husband and a father . if a 
 needless contest be prolong to the last , with all the irritation 
 it engender , who suffer ? - why , the tradesman and the operative . 
 Partiality , loss of custom , tyrannical demand for house rent , notice 
 to quit , - in a word , the screw ! " 

 " hear , hear ! " and " give we the ballot ! " 

 " the ballot - with all my heart , if I have it about I ! and if we have the 
 Ballot , I should like to see a man dare to vote Blue .   loud cheer from 
 the Yellows .   but , as we have not get it , we must think of our family . 
 and I may add , that though Mr. Egerton may come again into office , yet 
  add Dick solemnly   I will do my good , as his colleague , to keep he 
 straight ; and your own enlightenment   for the schoolmaster be abroad 
 will show he that no minister can brave public opinion , nor quarrel 
 with his own bread and butter .   much cheer .   in these time the 
 aristocracy must endear themselves to the middle and working class ; and 
 a member in office have much to give away in the Stamps and Excise , in 
 the Customs , the Post Office , and other State department in this 
 rotten old - I mean this magnificent empire , by which he can benefit his 
 constituent , and reconcile the prerogative of aristocracy with the 
 claim of the people , - more especially in this case , the people of the 
 borough of Lausmere .   hear , hear ! 

 " and therefore , sacrifice party inclination   since it seem that 
 I can in no way promote they   on the Altar of General Good Feeling , I 
 can not oppose the resignation of my nephew , - honourable relation ! - nor 
 blind my eye to the advantage that may result to a borough so 
 important to the nation at large , if the elector think fit to choose my 
 Right honourable brother - I mean the right honourable blue candidate - as 
 my brother colleague . not that I presume to dictate , or express a wish 
 one way or the other ; only , as a Family Man , I say to you , Electors and 
 Freemen , have serve your country in return I , you have nobly win 
 the right to think of the little one at home . " 

 Dick put his hand to his heart , bow gracefully , and retire from the 
 balcony amidst unanimous applause . 

 in three minute more Dick have resume his place in the booth in his 
 quality of candidate . a rush of Yellow elector pour in , hot and fast . 
 up come Emanuel Trout , and , in a firm voice , record his vote , " Avenel 
 and Egerton . " every man of the Hundred and Fifty so poll . to each 
 question , " whom do you vote for ? " " Avenel and Egerton " knell on the 
 ear of randal Leslie with " damnable iteration . " the young man fold 
 his arm across his breast in dogged despair . Levy have to shake hand 
 for Mr. Egerton with a rapidity that take away his breath . he long to 
 slink away , - long to get at L'Estrange , whom he suppose would be 
 as wroth at this turn in the wheel of fortune as himself . but how , as 
 Egerton 's representative , escape from the continuous gripe of those 
 horny hand ? besides , there stand the parish pump , right in face of the 
 booth , and some huge truculent - look yellow loiter round it , as 
 if ready to pounce on he the instant he quit his present sanctuary . 
 suddenly the crowd round the booth recede ; Lord L'Estrange 's carriage 
 drive up to the spot , and Harley , step from it , assist out of the 
 vehicle an old , gray - haired , paralytic man . the old man star round 
 he , and nod smilingly to the mob . " I be here,-I'm come ; I be but a 
 poor creature , but I be a good Blue to the last ! " 

 " Old John Avenel , - fine old John ! " cry many a voice . 

 and John Avenel , still lean on Harley 's arm , totter into the booth , 
 and plump for " Egerton . " 

 " shake hand , Father , " say Dick , bend forward , " though you will not 
 vote for I . " 

 " I be a Blue before you be bear , " answer the old man , tremulously ; 
 " but I wish you success all the same , and God bless you , my boy ! " 

 even the poll - clerk be touch ; and when Dick , leave his place , be 
 see by the crowd assist Lord L'Estrange to place poor John again 
 in the carriage , that picture of family love in the midst of political 
 difference - of the prosperous , wealthy , energetic son , who , as a boy , 
 have play at marble in the very kennel , and who have rise in life 
 by his own exertion , and be now virtually M. P. for his native town , 
 tend on the break - down , aged father , whom even the interest of 
 a son he be so proud of could not win from the colour which he 
 associate with truth and rectitude - have such an effect upon the rude 
 of the mob there present , that you might have hear a pin fall , - till 
 the carriage drive away back to John 's humble home ; and then there rise 
 such a tempest of huzzas ! John Avenel 's vote for Egerton give another 
 turn to the vicissitude of that memorable election . as yet Avenel have 
 be ahead of Audley ; but a plumper in favour of Egerton , from Avenel 's 
 own father , set an example and give an excuse to many a Blue who have not 
 yet vote , and could not prevail on himself to split his vote between 
 Dick and Audley ; and , therefore , several lead tradesman , who , see 
 that Egerton be safe , have previously resolve not to vote at all , come 
 up in the last hour , plump for Egerton , and carry he to the head 
 of the poll ; so that poor John , whose vote , involve that of Mark 
 Fairfield , have secure the first opening in public life to the young 
 ambition of the unknown son - in - law , still contribute to connect with 
 success and triumph , but also with sorrow , and , it may be , with death , 
 the name of the high - bear Egerton and the humble Avenel . 

 the great town - clock strike the hour of four ; the return officer 
 declare the poll close ; the formal announcement of the result will 
 be make later . but all the town know that Audley Egerton and Richard 
 Avenel be the member for Lausmere . and flag stream , and drum beat , 
 and man shake each other by the hand heartily ; and there be talk of the 
 chairing to - morrow ; and the public - house be crowd ; and there be an 
 indistinct hubbub in street and alley , with sudden burst of uproarious 
 shouting ; and the cloud to the west look red and lurid round the sun , 
 which have go down behind the church tower , - behind the yew - tree that 
 overshadow the quiet grave of Nora Avenel . 




 CHAPTER XXXIII . 

 amidst the darken shadow of twilight , Randal Leslie walk through 
 Lansmere Park towards the house . he have slink away before the poll be 
 closed , - creep through bylane , and plunge into the leafless copse 
 of the earl 's stately pasture - ground . amidst the bewilderment of 
 his thought - at a loss to conjecture how this strange mischance have 
 befall he , incline to ascribe it to Leonard 's influence over Avenel , 
 but suspect Harley , and half doubtful of Baron Levy - he seek to 
 ascertain what fault of judgment he himself have commit , what wile he 
 have forget , what thread in his web he have leave ragged and incomplete . 
 he could discover none . his ability seem to he unimpeachable , - totus , 
 tere , atque rotunda . and then there come across his breast a sharp 
 pang , - sharp than that of baffled ambition , - the feeling that he have 
 be deceive and bubble and betray . for so vital a necessity to all 
 live man be truth , that the vile traitor feel amazed and wrong , 
 feel the pillar of the world shake , when treason recoil on himself . 
 " that Richard Avenel , whom I trust , could so deceive I ! " murmur 
 Randal , and his lip quiver . 

 he be still in the midst of the Park , when a man with a yellow cockade 
 in his hat , and run fast from the direction of the town , overtook 
 he with a letter , on deliver which the messenger , wait for no 
 answer , hasten back the way he have come . randal recognize Avenel 's 
 hand on the address , break the seal , and read as follow : 

   Private and Confidential . 

  DEAR leslie , - do not   be down - hearte , - you will know to - night or 
  to - morrow why I have have cause to alter my opinion as to the right 
  Honourable ; and you will see that I could not , as a Family Man , act 
  otherwise than I have do . though I have not break my word to 
  you , - for you remember that all the help I promise be dependent on 
  my own resignation , and would go for nothing if Leonard resign 
  instead , - yet I feel you must think yourself rather bamboozle . but 
  I have be oblige to sacrifice you , from a sense of Family Duty , 
  as you will soon acknowledge . my own nephew be sacrifice also ; and 
  I have sacrifice my own concern , which require the whole man of I 
  for the next year or two at Screwstown . so we be all in the same 
  boat , though you may think you be set adrift by yourself . but I 
  do not   mean to stay in parliament . I shall take the Chiltern 
  Hundreds , pretty considerable soon . and if you keep well with the 
  Blues , I will do my good with the yellow to let you walk over the 
  course in my stead . for I do not   think Leonard will want to stand 
  again . and so a word to the wise , - and you may yet be member for 
  Lansmere . 

  R. A. 

 in this letter , randal , despite all his acuteness , could not detect the 
 honest compunction of the writer . he could at first only look at the 
 bad side of human nature , and fancy that it be a paltry attempt 
 to stifle his just anger and ensure his discretion ; but , on second 
 thought , it strike he that Dick might very naturally be glad to be 
 release to his mill , and get a quid pro quo out of Randal , under the 
 comprehensive title , " repayment of expense . " perhaps Dick be not 
 sorry to wait until Randal 's marriage give he the mean to make the 
 repayment . Nay , perhaps Randal have be throw over for the present , 
 in order to wring from he well term in a single election . thus 
 reasoning , he take comfort from his belief in the mercenary motif of 
 another . true ; it might be but a short disappointment . before the next 
 parliament be a month old , he might yet take his seat in it as member 
 for Lansmere . but all would depend on his marriage with the heiress ; he 
 must hasten that . 

 meanwhile , it be necessary to knit and gather up all his thought , 
 courage , and presence of mind . how he shrink from return to Lansmere 
 House , - from face Egerton , Harley , all . but there be no choice . he 
 would have to make it up with the Blues , - to defend the course he have 
 adopt in the Committee - room . there , no doubt , be Squire Hazeldean 
 await he with the purchase - money for the land of Rood ; there 
 be the Duke di Serrano , restore to wealth and honour ; there be his 
 promise bride , the great heiress , on whom depend all that could 
 raise the needy gentleman into wealth and position . gradually , with the 
 elastic temper that be essential to a systematic schemer , Randal Leslie 
 pluck himself from the pain of brood over a plot that be defeat , 
 to prepare himself for consummate those that yet seem so near 
 success . after all , should he fail in regain Egerton 's favour , 
 Egerton be of use no more . he might rear his head , and face out what 
 some might call " ingratitude , " provide he could but satisfy the Blue 
 Committee . dull dog , how could he fail to do that ! he could easily 
 talk over the machiavellian sage . he should have small difficulty in 
 explain all to the content of Audley 's distant brother , the squire . 
 Harley alone - but Levy have so positively assure he that Harley be not 
 sincerely anxious for Egerton ; and as to the more important explanation 
 relative to Peschiera , surely what have satisfy Violante 's father ought 
 to satisfy a man who have no peculiar right to demand explanation at 
 all ; and if these explanation do not satisfy , the onus to disprove 
 they must rest with Harley ; and who or what could contradict Randal 's 
 plausible assertion , - assertion in support of which he himself could 
 summon a witness in Baron Levy ? thus nerve himself to all that could 
 task his power , Randal Leslie cross the threshold of Lansmere House , 
 and in the hall he find the baron await he . 

 " I can not   account , " say Levy , " for what have go so cross in this 
 confound election . it be L'Estrange that puzzle I ; but I know that 
 he hat Egerton . I know that he will prove that hate by one mode of 
 revenge , if he have lose it in another . but it be well , Randal , that 
 you be secure of Hazeldean 's money and the rich heiress 's hand ; 
 otherwise - " 

 " otherwise , what ? " 

 " I should wash my hand of you , mon cher ; for , in spite of all your 
 cleverness , and all I have try to do for you , somehow or other I 
 begin to suspect that your talent will never secure your fortune . a 
 carpenter 's son beat you in public speaking , and a vulgar mill - owner 
 trick you in private negotiation . decidedly , as yet , Randal Leslie , you 
 be - a failure . and , as you so admirably say , ' a man from whom we have 
 nothing to hope or fear we must blot out of the map of the future . ' " 

 Randal 's answer be cut short by the appearance of the groom of the 
 chamber . 

 " my Lord be in the saloon , and request you and Mr. Leslie will do he 
 the honour to join he there . " the two gentleman follow the servant up 
 the broad stair . 

 the saloon form the centre room of the suite of apartment . from its 
 size , it be rarely use save on state occasion . it have the chilly and 
 formal aspect of room reserve for ceremony . 

 Riccabocca , Violante , Helen , Mr. Dale , Squire Hazeldean , and Lord 
 L'Estrange be group together by the cold florentine marble table , 
 not litter with book and female work , and the endear sign of 
 habitation , that give a living smile to the face of home ; nothing 
 thereon save a great silver candelabrum , that scarcely light the 
 spacious room , and bring out the portrait on the wall as a part of 
 the assembly , look , as portrait do look , with search , curious 
 eye upon every eye that turn to they . 

 but as soon as Randal enter , the squire detach himself from the 
 group , and , come to the defeat candidate , shake hand with he 
 heartily . 

 " cheer up , my boy ; ' t be no shame to be beat . Lord L'Estrange say you 
 do your good to win , and man can do no more . and I be glad , Leslie , that 
 we do not   meet for our little business till the election be over ; for , 
 after annoyance , something pleasant be twice as acceptable . I have   the 
 money in my pocket . hush ! and I say , my dear , dear boy , I can not find 
 out where Frank be , but it be really all off with that foreign woman , 
 eh ? " 

 " yes , indeed , sir , I hope so . I will talk to you about it when we can be 
 alone . we may slip away presently , I trust . " 

 " I will tell you a secret scheme of mine and Harry 's , " say the squire , in 
 a still low whisper . " we , must drive that marchioness , or whatever 
 she be , out of the boy 's head , and put a pretty english girl into it 
 instead . that will settle he in life too . and I must try and swallow 
 that bitter pill of the post - obit . Harry make bad of it than I do , 
 and be so hard on the poor fellow that I have   be oblige to take his 
 part . I have   no idea of be under petticoat government , it be not the 
 way with the Hazeldeans . well , but to come back to the point : whom do 
 you think I mean by the pretty girl ? " 

 " Miss Sticktorights ? " 

 " Zounds , no ! - your own little sister , Randal . sweet pretty face ! Harry 
 like she from the first , and then you will be Frank 's brother , and your 
 sound head and good heart will keep he right . and as you be go to 
 be marry too   you must tell I all about that later , why , we shall 
 have two marriage , perhaps , in the family on the same day . " 

 Randal 's hand grasp the squire 's , and with an emotion of human 
 gratitude , - for we know that , hard to all else , he have natural feeling 
 for his fallen family ; and his neglect sister be the one be on 
 earth whom he might almost be say to love . with all his intellectual 
 disdain for honest simple Frank , he know no one in the world with whom 
 his young sister could be more secure and happy . transfer to the 
 roof , and improve by the active kindness , of Mrs. Hazeldean , bl 
 in the manly affection of one not too refined to censure her own 
 deficiency of education , what more could he ask for his sister , as he 
 picture she to himself , with her hair hang over her ear , and her 
 mind run into seed over some trashy novel . but before he could 
 reply , Violante 's father come to add his own philosophical consolation 
 to the squire 's downright comforting . 

 " who could ever count on popular caprice ? the wise of all age have 
 despise it . in that respect , Horace and Machiavelli be of the same 
 mind , " etc . " but , " say the duke , with emphatic kindness " perhaps your 
 very misfortune here may serve you elsewhere . the female heart be prone 
 to pity , and ever eager to comfort . besides , if I be recall to Italy , 
 you will have leisure to come with we , and see the land where , of all 
 other , ambition can be most readily forget , even " add the Italian 
 with a sigh - " even by her own son ! " 

 thus address by both Hazeldean and the duke , Randal recover his 
 spirit . it be clear that Lord L'Estrange have not convey to they 
 any unfavourable impression of his conduct in the Committee - room . while 
 Randal have be thus engage , Levy have make his way to Harley , who 
 retreat with the baron into the bay of the great window . 

 " well , my Lord , do you comprehend this conduct on the part of Richard 
 Avenel ? he secure Egerton 's return ! - he ! " 

 " what so natural , Baron Levy , - his own brother - in - law ? " the baron 
 start , and turn very pale . 

 " but how do he know that ? I never tell he . I mean indeed - " 

 " Meant , perhaps , to shame Egerton 's pride at the last by publicly 
 declare his marriage with a shopkeeper 's daughter . a very good revenge 
 still leave to you ; but revenge for what ? a word with you , now , Baron , 
 that our acquaintance be about to close forever . you know why I have 
 cause for resentment against Egerton . I do but suspect your ; will you 
 make it clear to I ? " 

 " my Lord , my Lord , " falter Baron Levy , " I , too , woo Nora Avenel as 
 my wife ; I , too , have a happy rival in the haughty worldling who do 
 not appreciate his own felicity ; I too - in a word , some woman inspire an 
 affection that mingle with the entire being of a man , and be fuse with 
 all the current of his life - blood . Nora Avenel be one of those woman . " 

 Harley be startle . this burst of emotion from a man so corrupt and 
 cynical arrest even the scorn he feel for the usurer . Levy soon 
 recover himself . " but our revenge be not baffle yet . Egerton , if not 
 already in my power , be still in your . his election may save he from 
 arrest , but the law have other mode of public exposure and effectual 
 ruin . " 

 " for the knave , yes , - as I intimate to you in your own house , - you who 
 boast of your love to Nora Avenel , and know in your heart that you be 
 her destroyer ; you who witness her marriage , and yet dare to tell she 
 that she be dishonour ! " 

 " my Lord - I - how could you know - I mean , how think that - that - " 
 falter Levy , aghast . 

 " Nora Avenel have speak from her grave , " reply Harley , solemnly . 
 " learn that , wherever man commit a crime , Heaven find a witness ! " 

 " it be on I , then , " say Levy , wrestle against a superstitious thrill 
 at his heart - " on I that you now concentre your vengeance ; and I must 
 meet it as I may . but I have fulfil my part of our compact . I have 
 obey you implicitly - and - " 

 " I will fulfil my part of our bond , and leave you undisturbed in your 
 wealth . " 

 " I know I might trust to your Lordship 's honour , " exclaim the usurer , 
 in servile glee . 

 " and this vile creature nurse the same passion as myself ; and but 
 yesterday we be partner in the same purpose , and influence by the 
 same thought ! " mutter Harley to himself . " yes , " he say aloud , " I 
 dare not , Baron Levy , constitute myself your judge . pursue your own 
 path , - all road meet at last before the common tribunal . but you be 
 not yet release from our compact ; you must do some good in spite of 
 yourself . look yonder , where randal Leslie stand , smile secure , 
 between the two danger he have raise up for himself . and as randal 
 Leslie himself have invite I to be his judge , and you be aware that 
 he cite yourself this very day as his witness , here I must expose the 
 guilty ; for here the innocent still live , and need defence . " 

 Harley turn away , and take his place by the table . " I have wish , " 
 say he , raise his voice , " to connect with the triumph of my early 
 and dearest friend the happiness of other in whose welfare I feel an 
 interest . to you , Alphonso , Duke of Serrano , I now give this despatch , 
 receive last evening by a special messenger from the Prince Von - - - , 
 announce your restoration to your land and honour . " 

 the squire star with open mouth . " Rickeybockey a duke ? why , Jemima 's a 
 duchess ! bless I , she be actually cry ! " and his good heart prompt 
 he to run to his cousin and cheer she up a bit . 

 Violante glance at Harley , and fling herself on her father 's breast . 
 randal involuntarily rise , and move to the duke 's chair . 

 " and you , Mr. Randal Leslie , " continue Harley , " though you have lose 
 your election , see before you at this moment such prospect of wealth 
 and happiness , that I shall only have to offer you congratulation to 
 which those that greet Mr. Audley Egerton may well appear lukewarm and 
 insipid , provide you prove that you have not forfeit the right to 
 claim that promise which the Duke di Serrano have accord to the 
 suitor of his daughter 's hand . some doubt rest on my mind , you have 
 volunteer to dispel they . I have the duke 's permission to address to 
 you a few question , and I now avail myself of your offer to reply to 
 they . " 

 " now , - and here , my Lord ? " say Randal , glance round the room , as if

 " 's death ! " cry the squire , also gaze on Randal 's cower eye and

 a bombshell , descend through the roof could not have produce a more

 Craft against craft , talent against talent , treason against treason - in

 the contrast be strike , and imply that truth so universally feel ,

 adopt ! I think of the childless man when your Lordship imagine I 
 be but in fear of your threat . ha ! ha ! that will sting . "
